In practice, this repository should be understood as **IronClaude evolving from the SuperClaude codebase**.\n6\t\n7\t## What IronClaude does\n8\t\n9\tIronClaude provides a working framework for:\n10\t\n11\t- structured Claude Code commands, skills, and agents\n12\t- repeatable CLI pipelines for roadmap, tasklist, sprint, audit, and related workflows\n13\t- pytest-based validation and evaluation patterns\n14\t- evidence-first development with confidence checks and self-checking patterns\n15\t- MCP-assisted codebase analysis, research, and orchestration\n16\t\n17\t## Current naming reality\n18\t\n19\tThis repository currently has two layers of identity:\n20\t\n21\t- **Project identity**: IronClaude\n22\t- **Implementation/package identity**: `superclaude`\n23\t\n24\tThat means you will still see names like these in the codebase:\n25\t\n26\t- Python package: `superclaude`\n27\t- CLI command: `superclaude`\n28\t- source tree: `src/superclaude/`\n29\t- pytest plugin entry point: `superclaude.pytest_plugin`\n30\t\n31\tThe README reflects the product/function of the repo first, while preserving the current technical names contributors still need to use.\n32\t\n33\t## Highlights\n34\t\n35\t- **CLI-driven workflows** for roadmap, tasklist, sprint, audit, and validation\n36\t- **Claude Code asset distribution** through commands, agents, and skills\n37\t- **PM-agent patterns** for confidence, self-check, and reflexion\n38\t- **MCP-aware development** with integrations such as Auggie, Serena, Tavily, Context7, and Sequential\n39\t- **Contributor workflow support** with source/dev-mirror sync between `src/superclaude/` and `.claude/`\n40\t\n41\t## Repository purpose\n42\t\n43\tThis repository serves as:\n44\t\n45\t- the source of truth for IronClaude’s current workflow framework\n46\t- the Python package implementation that still ships as `superclaude`\n47\t- the home of the `superclaude` CLI\n48\t- the implementation of the auto-loaded pytest plugin\n49\t- the source for distributable Claude Code commands, agents, and skills\n50\t- a workspace for generated docs, release/process artifacts, and evaluation work\n51\t\n52\t## Installation\n53\t\n54\t### Install the current package/CLI\n55\t\n56\tThe install surface still uses the historical package name:\n57\t\n58\t```bash\n59\tpipx install superclaude\n60\tsuperclaude install\n61\t```\n62\t\n63\tThis installs the package and then installs its framework assets into your Claude Code directory.\n64\t\n65\t### Install from source for development\n66\t\n67\tThis repository uses **UV** for Python operations.\n68\t\n69\t```bash\n70\tgit clone https://github.com/IronbellyOrg/IronClaude\n71\tcd IronClaude\n72\tmake dev\n73\tmake verify\n74\t```\n75\t\n76\t## Quick start\n77\t\n78\t### Core CLI commands\n79\t\n80\t```bash\n81\tsuperclaude --version\n82\tsuperclaude doctor\n83\tsuperclaude install\n84\tsuperclaude mcp --list\n85\t```\n86\t\n87\t### Run workflow pipelines\n88\t\n89\t```bash\n90\tsuperclaude sprint run <tasklist-index.md>\n91\tsuperclaude roadmap run <spec.md>\n92\tsuperclaude roadmap validate <output-dir>\n93\tsuperclaude tasklist run <roadmap-dir>\n94\t```\n95\t\n96\t### Run tests\n97\t\n98\t```bash\n99\tmake test\n100\tuv run pytest tests/pm_agent/ -v\n101\tuv run pytest -m confidence_check\n102\t```\n103\t\n104\t## Core concepts\n105\t\n106\t### 1. CLI and pipelines\n107\t\n108\tThe active CLI entry point is `superclaude`, implemented in `src/superclaude/cli/main.py`.\n109\t\n110\tKey workflow areas include:\n111\t\n112\t- sprint execution\n113\t- roadmap generation and validation\n114\t- tasklist generation\n115\t- audit and cleanup audit flows\n116\t- MCP installation and diagnostics\n117\t\n118\t### 2. Pytest plugin\n119\t\n120\tThe package registers an auto-loaded pytest plugin via `pyproject.toml`.\n121\t\n122\tIt provides fixtures such as:\n123\t\n124\t- `confidence_checker`\n125\t- `self_check_protocol`\n126\t- `reflexion_pattern`\n127\t- `token_budget`\n128\t- `pm_context`\n129\t\n130\tIt also exposes workflow-oriented markers including `confidence_check`, `self_check`, and `reflexion`.\n131\t\n132\t### 3. PM-agent patterns\n133\t\n134\tA major part of IronClaude’s operating model lives in `src/superclaude/pm_agent/`:\n135\t\n136\t- **ConfidenceChecker** — assess confidence before implementation\n137\t- **SelfCheckProtocol** — validate implementation with evidence after changes\n138\t- **ReflexionPattern** — capture learning from errors and prevent repeats\n139\t\n140\t### 4. Source of truth vs dev mirrors\n141\t\n142\tFor distributable framework assets, **`src/superclaude/` remains the source of truth**.\n143\t\n144\tThe `.claude/` directory contains development mirrors used directly by Claude Code during local iteration. When editing commands, agents, or skills, update `src/superclaude/` first and then sync to `.claude/`.\n145\t\n146\t## Repository structure\n147\t\n148\t```text\n149\tsrc/superclaude/         Canonical Python package and framework source\n150\t├── cli/                 CLI commands and pipeline entrypoints\n151\t├── pm_agent/            Confidence, self-check, reflexion patterns\n152\t├── execution/           Parallel execution and reflection helpers\n153\t├── commands/            Slash command definitions\n154\t├── agents/              Agent definition source files\n155\t├── skills/              Skill packages and support assets\n156\t├── core/                Framework rules and principles\n157\t└── pytest_plugin.py     Auto-loaded pytest integration\n158\t\n159\t.claude/                 Dev mirrors used by Claude Code\n160\tplugins/                 Distribution/plugin artifacts\n161\tscripts/                 Build, analysis, and maintenance scripts\n162\ttests/                   Pytest suite and eval coverage\n163\tdocs/                    User, developer, reference, and generated docs\n164\t```\n165\t\n166\t## Development workflow\n167\t\n168\t### Common commands\n169\t\n170\t```bash\n171\tmake dev                # Install editable package + dev dependencies\n172\tmake verify             # Verify package, plugin, and health checks\n173\tmake test               # Run the full test suite\n174\tmake lint               # Run ruff checks\n175\tmake format             # Format code with ruff\n176\tmake doctor             # Run health diagnostics\n177\tmake sync-dev           # Sync src/superclaude/ -> .claude/\n178\tmake verify-sync        # import ExistingWorkState\n20\t\n21\t\n22\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n23\t# Existing Work Detection (FR-PRD.1)\n24\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n25\t\n26\t\n27\tdef check_existing_work(config: PrdConfig) -> ExistingWorkState:\n28\t    \"\"\"Detect pre-existing PRD work in the task directory tree.\n29\t\n30\t    Scans ``.dev/tasks/to-do/TASK-PRD-*/`` for matching product work.\n31\t    Returns one of four states:\n32\t\n33\t    - NO_EXISTING: No matching task directory found.\n34\t    - RESUME_STAGE_A: Task dir exists but no completed research files.\n35\t    - RESUME_STAGE_B: Research files exist; synthesis/assembly incomplete.\n36\t    - ALREADY_COMPLETE: Final PRD artifact present and passing.\n37\t\n38\t    F-008: Short product names (< 3 chars) match via frontmatter\n39\t    ``product_name`` field instead of full-content substring.\n40\t    \"\"\"\n41\t    tasks_root = config.work_dir / \".dev\" / \"tasks\" / \"to-do\"\n42\t    if not tasks_root.is_dir():\n43\t        return ExistingWorkState.NO_EXISTING\n44\t\n45\t    # Find matching TASK-PRD-* directories\n46\t    matching_dirs = _find_matching_task_dirs(\n47\t        tasks_root, config.product_name, config.product_slug\n48\t    )\n49\t    if not matching_dirs:\n50\t        return ExistingWorkState.NO_EXISTING\n51\t\n52\t    # Use most recently modified matching dir\n53\t    task_dir = max(matching_dirs, key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime)\n54\t\n55\t    # Check for final PRD artifact\n56\t    results_dir = task_dir / \"results\"\n57\t    if results_dir.is_dir():\n58\t        prd_files = list(results_dir.glob(\"*.md\"))\n59\t        if prd_files:\n60\t            return ExistingWorkState.ALREADY_COMPLETE\n61\t\n62\t    # Check for research files\n63\t    research_files = discover_research_files(task_dir)\n64\t    if not research_files:\n65\t        return ExistingWorkState.RESUME_STAGE_A\n66\t\n67\t    return ExistingWorkState.RESUME_STAGE_B\n68\t\n69\t\n70\tdef _find_matching_task_dirs(\n71\t    tasks_root: Path, product_name: str, product_slug: str\n72\t) -> list[Path]:\n73\t    \"\"\"Find TASK-PRD-* directories matching the product.\n74\t\n75\t    F-008: For short product names (< 3 chars), requires frontmatter\n76\t    product_name match rather than substring search to avoid false positives.\n77\t    \"\"\"\n78\t    matches: list[Path] = []\n79\t    for task_dir in tasks_root.glob(\"TASK-PRD-*\"):\n80\t        if not task_dir.is_dir():\n81\t            continue\n82\t\n83\t        # Check slug in directory name first\n84\t        dir_name = task_dir.name.lower()\n85\t        if product_slug and product_slug.lower() in dir_name:\n86\t            matches.append(task_dir)\n87\t            continue\n88\t\n89\t        # For short product names, require frontmatter match\n90\t        if len(product_name) < 3:\n91\t            if _frontmatter_matches(task_dir, product_name):\n92\t                matches.append(task_dir)\n93\t        else:\n94\t            # Full-content substring match for longer names\n95\t            if _content_matches(task_dir, product_name):\n96\t                matches.append(task_dir)\n97\t\n98\t    return matches\n99\t\n100\t\n101\tdef _frontmatter_matches(task_dir: Path, product_name: str) -> bool:\n102\t    \"\"\"Check if any markdown file in task_dir has matching product_name in frontmatter.\"\"\"\n103\t    for md_file in task_dir.glob(\"*.md\"):\n104\t        try:\n105\t            content = md_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")[:2000]\n106\t            fm_match = re.search(\n107\t                r\"^---\\s*\\n(.*?)\\n---\", content, re.DOTALL\n108\t            )\n109\t            if fm_match:\n110\t                fm_text = fm_match.group(1)\n111\t                name_match = re.search(\n112\t                    r\"product_name\\s*:\\s*(.+)\", fm_text, re.IGNORECASE\n113\t                )\n114\t                if name_match and Above the threshold, splits into\n29\t    roughly even partitions where each partition has at most ``threshold``\n30\t    items.\n31\t\n32\t    Args:\n33\t        files: List of file paths (or any items) to partition.\n34\t        threshold: Maximum items per partition.\n35\t\n36\t    Returns:\n37\t        A list of partitions (each partition is a list of items).\n38\t        Returns an empty list if files is empty.\n39\t    \"\"\"\n40\t    if not files:\n41\t        return []\n42\t    if len(files) <= threshold:\n43\t        return [list(files)]\n44\t\n45\t    num_partitions = math.ceil(len(files) / threshold)\n46\t    partition_size = math.ceil(len(files) / num_partitions)\n47\t\n48\t    partitions: list[list] = []\n49\t    for i in range(0, len(files), partition_size):\n50\t        partitions.append(list(files[i : i + partition_size]))\n51\t    return partitions\n52\t\n53\t\n54\t#   - ``- [ ] Gap: description``\n66\t    - Lines under a ``## Gap Analysis`` or ``## Gaps`` heading\n67\t\n68\t    Returns a deduplicated list of gap dictionaries with keys:\n69\t    ``description``, ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n130\t# QA Partition Report Merging (GAP-005)\n131\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n132\t\n133\t\n134\tdef merge_qa_partition_reports(reports: list[dict]) -> dict:\n135\t    \"\"\"Merge multiple QA partition    reports: List of dicts, each with at least a ``verdict`` key\n142\t                 (\"PASS\" or \"FAIL\") and optional ``issues`` list.\n143\t\n144\t    Returns:\n145\t        Merged report dict \"verdict\": \"PASS\",\n151\t            \"partition_count\": 0,\n152\t            \"partitions_passed\": 0,\n153\t            \"partitions_failed\": 0,\n154\t            \"issues\": [],\n155\t        }\n156\t\n157\t    all_issues: list[str] = []\n158\t    passed = 0\n159\t    failed = 0\n160\t\n161\t    for report in reports:\n162\t        verdict = report.get(\"verdict\", \"FAIL\").upper()\n163\t Classification priority:\n164\t        1. STALL: watchdog triggered or high stall duration\n165\t        2. TIMEOUT: exit code 124 (process timeout)\n166\t        3. CRASH: non-zero exit with low stall (subprocess died)\n167\t        4. ERROR: result file indicates error/halt\n168\t        5. UNKNOWN: no clear evidence\n169\t        \"\"\"\n170\t        evidence = []\n171\t        status = bundle.phase_result.status\n172\t        exit_code = bundle.phase_result.exit_code\n173\t\n174\t        # 1. Stall detection\n175\t        if bundle.watchdog_triggered:\n176\t            evidence.append(\"Watchdog triggered — stall detected by timeout\")\n177\t            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n178\t            return FailureCategory.STALL\n179\t\n180\t        if bundle.stall_duration > 120:\n181\t            evidence.append(f\"High stall duration: {bundle.stall_duration:.0f}s\")\n182\t            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n183\t            return FailureCategory.STALL\n184\t\n185\t        # 2. Timeout\n186\t        if status == PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT or exit_code == 124:\n187\t            evidence.append(f\"Process timeout (exit code {exit_code})\")\n188\t            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n189\t            return FailureCategory.TIMEOUT\n190\t\n191\t        # 2.5. Context exhaustion (prompt too long)\n192\t        from .monitor import detect_prompt_too_long\n193\t\n194\t        if bundle.config is not None:\n195\t            output_file = bundle.config.output_file(bundle.phase_result.phase)\n196\t        else:\n197\t            import warnings\n198\t\n199\t            warnings.warn(\n200\t                \"DiagnosticBundle.config=None is deprecated; pass SprintConfig\",\n201\t                DeprecationWarning,\n202\t                stacklevel=2,\n203\t            )\n204\t            output_file = (\n205\t                bundle.phase_result.phase.file.parent.parent\n206\t                / \"results\"\n207\t                / f\"phase-{bundle.phase_result.phase.number}-output.txt\"\n208\t            )\n209\t\n210\t        if exit_code != 0 and detect_prompt_too_long(output_file):\n211\t            evidence.append(f\"Context exhaustion detected (exit code {exit_code})\")\n212\t            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n213\t            return FailureCategory.CONTEXT_EXHAUSTION\n214\t\n215\t        # 3. Crash (non-zero exit, not timeout, low stall)\n216\t        if exit_code != 0 and bundle.stall_duration < 30:\n217\t            evidence.append(\n218\t                f\"Process crash (exit code {exit_code}, stall {bundle.stall_duration:.0f}s)\"\n219\t            )\n220\t            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n221\t            return FailureCategory.CRASH\n222\t\n223\t        # 4. Error (result file signals)\n224\t        if status in (PhaseStatus.HALT, PhaseStatus.ERROR):\n225\t            evidence.append(f\"Phase status: {status.value}\")\n226\t            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n227\t            return FailureCategory.ERROR\n228\t\n229\t        # 5. Unknown\n230\t        evidence.append(\"No clear failure pattern identified\")\n231\t        bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n232\t        return FailureCategory.UNKNOWN\n233\t\n234\t\n235\tclass ReportGenerator:\n236\t    \"\"\"Generates human-readable diagnostic reports.\"\"\"\n237\t\n238\t    def generate(self, bundle: DiagnosticBundle) -> str:\n239\t        \"\"\"Generate a structured diagnostic report from a bundle.\"\"\"\n240\t        lines = [\n241\t            f\"# Diagnostic Report — Phase {bundle.phase.number}\",\n242\t            \"\",\n243\t            \"## Summary\",\n244\t            f\"- **Category**: {bundle.category.value}\",\n245\t            f\"- **Status**: {bundle.phase_result.status.value}\",\n246\t            f\"- {bundle.stall_duration:.0f}s\",\n249\t            f\"- **Watchdog Triggered**: {bundle.watchdog_triggered}\",\n250\t            \"\",\n251\t            \"## Evidence\",\n252\t        ]\n253\t\n254\t        for ev in bundle.classification_evidence:\n255\t            lines.append(f\"- {ev}\")\n256\t\n257\t        if bundle.monitor_state_snapshot:\n258\t            lines.append(\"\")\n259\t            lines.append(\"## Monitor State\")\n260\t            for k, v in bundle.monitor_state_snapshot.items():\n261\t                lines.append(f\"- **{k}**: {v}\")\n262\t\n263\t        if bundle.last_events:\n264\t            lines.append(\"\")\n265\t            lines.append(\"## Last Debug Events\")\n266\t            lines.append(\"```\")\n267\t            for entry in bundle.last_events[-10:]:\n268\t                lines.append(entry)\n269\t            lines.append(\"```\")\n270\t\n271\t        if bundle.output_tail:\n272\t            lines.append(\"\")\n273\t            lines.append(\"## Output Tail\")\n274\t            lines.append(\"```\")\n275\t            lines.append(bundle.output_tail)\n276\t            lines.append(\"```\")\n277\t\n278\t        if lines.append(bundle.stderr_tail)\n283\t            lines.append(\"```\")\n284\t\n285\t        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n286\t\n287\t    def write(self, bundle: DiagnosticBundle, output_path: Path) Content of `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/prd/gates.py`\n\n```python\n\"\"\"PRD pipeline gate criteria -- semantic checks and step-to-gate mappings.\n\nDefines the GATE_CRITERIA dict mapping each PRD pipeline step to its\nGateCriteria instance, plus 10 semantic check functions organized in\ntwo layers:\n\n  1. Reusable checks: _check_verdict_field, _check_no_placeholders\n  2. PRD-specific checks: _check_parsed_request_fields,\n     _check_research_notes_sections, _check_suggested_phases_detail,\n     _check_task_phases_present, _check_b2_self_contained,\n     _check_parallel_instructions, _check_prd_template_sections,\n     _check_qa_verdict\n\nAll semantic check functions follow Callable[[str], bool | str] signature:\n  - Return True on pass\n  - Return an error string on failure\n\nAll checks are wrapped in try/except: exceptions return\n(False, \"check '{name}' crashed: {error}\") per F-005.\n\nNFR-PRD.2: All _check_* functions match Callable[[str], bool | str].\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport re\nfrom typing import Callable\n\nfrom superclaude.cli.pipeline.models import GateCriteria, SemanticCheck\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Layer 1: Reusable semantic checks\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _check_verdict_field(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that content contains a verdict field set to PASS or FAIL.\n\n    Accepts both JSON format (\"verdict\": \"PASS\") and markdown format\n    (verdict: PASS or **Verdict**: PASS).\n    \"\"\"\n    # JSON format\n    json_match = re.search(r'\"verdict\"\\s*:\\s*\"(PASS|FAIL)\"', content)\n    if json_match:\n        return True\n    # Markdown format (case-insensitive key, case-sensitive value)\n    md_match = re.search(\n        r\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*\\*{0,2}[Vv]erdict\\*{0,2}\\s*:\\s*(PASS|FAIL)\",\n        content,\n    )\n    if md_match:\n        return True\n    return \"No verdict field found (expected 'verdict: PASS' or 'verdict: FAIL')\"\n\n\ndef _check_no_placeholders(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that content contains no placeholder text.\n\n    Catches TODO, TBD, PLACEHOLDER, [INSERT], and similar markers.\n    \"\"\"\n    patterns = [\n        (r\"\\bTODO\\b\", \"TODO\"),\n        (r\"\\bTBD\\b\", \"TBD\"),\n        (r\"\\bPLACEHOLDER\\b\", \"PLACEHOLDER\"),\n        (r\"\\[INSERT[^\\]]*\\]\", \"[INSERT...]\"),\n        (r\"\\[FILL[^\\]]*\\]\", \"[FILL...]\"),\n    ]\n    found = []\n    for pattern, label in patterns:\n        matches = re.findall(pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if matches:\n            found.append(f\"{label} ({len(matches)}x)\")\n    if found:\n        return f\"Placeholder text found: {', '.join(found)}\"\n    return True\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Layer 2: PRD-specific semantic checks\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _check_parsed_request_fields(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that parsed request contains GOAL, PRODUCT_SLUG, PRD_SCOPE, SCENARIO.\"\"\"\n    required = [\"GOAL\", \"PRODUCT_SLUG\", \"PRD_SCOPE\", \"SCENARIO\"]\n    missing = []\n    for field_name in required:\n        # Check JSON key format\n        json_pat = re.compile(rf'\"{field_name}\"\\s*:\\s*\"[^\"]+\"', re.IGNORECASE)\n        # Check markdown key: value format\n        md_pat = re.compile(\n            rf\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*\\*{{0,2}}{field_name}\\*{{0,2}}\\s*:\\s*\\S\",\n            re.IGNORECASE,\n        )\n        if not json_pat.search(content) and not md_pat.search(content):\n            missing.append(field_name)\n    if missing:\n        return f\"Missing required fields: {', '.join(missing)}\"\n    return True\n\n\n_RESEARCH_REQUIRED_SECTIONS = [\n    \"Product Capabilities\",\n    \"Technical Architecture\",\n    \"User Flows\",\n    \"Integration Points\",\n    \"Existing Documentation\",\n    \"Gap Analysis\",\n    \"Suggested Phases\",\n]\n\n\ndef _check_research_notes_sections(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that research notes contain all 7 required sections.\"\"\"\n    missing = []\n    for section in _RESEARCH_REQUIRED_SECTIONS:\n        # Match as markdown heading (## or ###) or bold text\n        heading_pat = re.compile(\n            rf\"^\\s*#{{1,4}}\\s+.*{re.escape(section)}\", re.MULTILINE | re.IGNORECASE\n        )\n        bold_pat = re.compile(\n            rf\"\\*\\*{re.escape(section)}\\*\\*\", re.IGNORECASE\n        )\n        if not heading_pat.search(content) and not bold_pat.search(content):\n            missing.append(section)\n    if missing:\n        return f\"Missing research sections: {', '.join(missing)}\"\n    return True\n\n\ndef _check_suggested_phases_detail(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that the Suggested Phases section contains per-agent detail.\n\n    Expects at least one numbered or bulleted list item under a Phases heading.\n    \"\"\"\n    phases_match = re.search(\n        r\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*#{1,4}\\s+.*(?:Suggested\\s+)?Phases\",\n        content,\n        re.IGNORECASE,\n    )\n    if not phases_match:\n        return \"No 'Suggested Phases' section found\"\n    # Check for list items after the heading\n    after_heading = content[phases_match.end() :]\n    list_pat = re.search(r\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*(?:\\d+\\.|[-*])\\s+\\S\", after_heading)\n    if not list_pat:\n        return \"Suggested Phases section has no detail items\"\n    return True\n\n\ndef _check_task_phases_present(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that the task file contains phase definitions.\n\n    Expects headings like 'Phase 1:', 'Phase 2:', etc.\n    \"\"\"\n    phase_headings = re.findall(\n        r\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*#{1,4}\\s+.*Phase\\s+\\d\", content, re.IGNORECASE\n    )\n    if len(phase_headings) < 2:\n        return f\"Expected multiple phase headings, found {len(phase_headings)}\"\n    return True\n\n\ndef _check_b2_self_contained(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that checklist items do not reference external content.\n\n    Catches 'see above', 'as mentioned', 'refer to' violations in\n    checklist items (lines starting with '- [ ]' or '- [x]').\n    \"\"\"\n    violations = []\n    for match in re.finditer(\n        r\"^\\s*-\\s+\\[[ x]\\]\\s+(.+)$\", content, re.MULTILINE\n    ):\n        item_text = match.group(1)\n        for phrase in [\"see above\", \"as mentioned\", \"refer to\", \"as described\"]:\n            if phrase.lower() in item_text.lower():\n                violations.append(f\"'{phrase}' in: {item_text[:60]}\")\n    if violations:\n        return (\n            f\"Self-containment violations in checklist items: \"\n            f\"{'; '.join(violations[:3])}\"\n        )\n    return True\n\n\ndef _check_parallel_instructions(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that phases 2-5 contain parallel execution keywords.\n\n    Validates that later phases include 'parallel', 'concurrent',\n    'simultaneously', or 'batch' instructions.\n    \"\"\"\n    parallel_keywords = [\"parallel\", \"concurrent\", \"simultaneously\", \"batch\"]\n    # Find phase sections 2+\n    phase_sections = list(\n        re.finditer(\n            r\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*#{1,4}\\s+.*Phase\\s+(\\d+)\",\n            content,\n            re.IGNORECASE,\n        )\n    )\n    later_phases = [m for m in phase_sections if int(m.group(1)) >= 2]\n    if not start = phase_match.end()\n        end = (\n            later_phases[i + 1].start()\n            if i + 1 < len(later_phases)\n            else len(content)\n        )\n        section_text = content[start:end].lower()\n        if not any(kw in section_text for kw in parallel_keywords):\n            phase_num = phase_match.group(1)\n            return (\n                f\"Phase \"Executive Summary\",\n    \"Problem Statement\",\n    \"Technical Requirements\",\n    \"Implementation Plan\",\n    \"Success Metrics\",\n]\n\n\ndef _check_prd_template_sections(content: str) -> return f\"Missing PRD sections: {', '.join(missing)}\"\n    return True\n\n\ndef _check_qa_verdict(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that QA report contains a verdict of PASS or FAIL.\"\"\"\n    return _check_verdict_field(content)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Safe wrapper for semantic checks (F-005)\n# function so exceptions become error strings.\"\"\"\n\n    def wrapper(content: str) -> bool | str:\n        try:\n            return fn(content)\n        except Exception as exc:\n            return Content of `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/prd/inventory.py`\n\n```python\n\"\"\"PRD pipeline inventory -- file discovery and existing work detection.\n\nPure-function module for scanning task directories, detecting existing\nPRD work state, and selecting templates. All functions are side-effect-free\nexcept ``create_task_dirs`` which creates subdirectories.\n\nDependencies: models (ExistingWorkState, PrdConfig)\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import TYPE_CHECKING\n\nif TYPE_CHECKING:\n    from .models import PrdConfig\n\nfrom .models import ExistingWorkState\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Existing Work Detection (FR-PRD.1)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef check_existing_work(config: PrdConfig) -> ExistingWorkState:\n    \"\"\"Detect pre-existing PRD work in the task directory tree.\n\n    Scans ``.dev/tasks/to-do/TASK-PRD-*/`` for matching product work.\n    Returns one of four states:\n\n    - NO_EXISTING: No matching task directory found.\n    - RESUME_STAGE_A: Task dir exists but no completed research files.\n    - RESUME_STAGE_B: Research files exist; synthesis/assembly incomplete.\n    - ALREADY_COMPLETE: Final PRD artifact present and passing.\n\n    F-008: Short product names (< 3 chars) match via frontmatter\n    ``product_name`` field instead of full-content substring.\n    \"\"\"\n    tasks_root = config.work_dir / \".dev\" / \"tasks\" / \"to-do\"\n    if not tasks_root.is_dir():\n        return ExistingWorkState.NO_EXISTING\n\n    # Find matching TASK-PRD-* directories\n    matching_dirs = _find_matching_task_dirs(\n        tasks_root, config.product_name, config.product_slug\n    )\n    if not matching_dirs:\n        return ExistingWorkState.NO_EXISTING\n\n    # Use most recently modified matching dir\n    task_dir = max(matching_dirs, key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime)\n\n    # Check for final PRD artifact\n    results_dir = task_dir / \"results\"\n    if results_dir.is_dir():\n        prd_files = list(results_dir.glob(\"*.md\"))\n        if prd_files:\n            return ExistingWorkState.ALREADY_COMPLETE\n\n    # Check for research files\n    research_files = discover_research_files(task_dir)\n    if not research_files:\n        return ExistingWorkState.RESUME_STAGE_A\n\n    return ExistingWorkState.RESUME_STAGE_B\n\n\ndef _find_matching_task_dirs(\n    tasks_root: Path, product_name: str, product_slug: str\n) -> list[Path]:\n    \"\"\"Find TASK-PRD-* directories matching the product.\n\n    F-008: For short product names (< 3 chars), requires frontmatter\n    product_name match rather than substring search to avoid false positives.\n    \"\"\"\n    matches: list[Path] = []\n    for task_dir in tasks_root.glob(\"TASK-PRD-*\"):\n        if not task_dir.is_dir():\n            continue\n\n        # Check slug in directory name first\n        dir_name = task_dir.name.lower()\n        if product_slug and product_slug.lower() in dir_name:\n            matches.append(task_dir)\n            continue\n\n        # For short product names, require frontmatter match\n        if len(product_name) < 3:\n            if _frontmatter_matches(task_dir, product_name):\n                matches.append(task_dir)\n        else:\n            # Full-content substring match for longer names\n            if _content_matches(task_dir, product_name):\n                matches.append(task_dir)\n\n    return matches\n\n\ndef _frontmatter_matches(task_dir: Path, product_name: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if any markdown file in task_dir has matching product_name in frontmatter.\"\"\"\n    for md_file in task_dir.glob(\"*.md\"):\n        try:\n            content = md_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")[:2000]\n            fm_match = re.search(\n                r\"^---\\s*\\n(.*?)\\n---\", content, re.DOTALL\n            )\n            if fm_match:\n                fm_text = fm_match.group(1)\n                name_match = re.search(\n                    r\"product_name\\s*:\\s*(.+)\", fm_text, re.IGNORECASE\n                )\n                if name_match and name_match.group(1).strip().lower() == product_name.lower():\n                    return True\n        except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError):\n            continue\n    return False\n\n\ndef _content_matches(task_dir: Path, product_name: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if any markdown file in task_dir contains the product name.\"\"\"\n    for md_file in task_dir.glob(\"*.md\"):\n        try:\n            content = md_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")[:5000]\n            if product_name.lower() in content.lower():\n                return True\n        except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError):\n            continue\n    return False\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# File Discovery\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef discover_research_files(task_dir: Path) -> list[Path]:\n    \"\"\"Find completed research files in the task directory.\n\n    Scans ``research/*.md`` and returns only files that are considered\n    complete (non-empty and not containing incomplete markers).\n    \"\"\"\n    research_dir = task_dir / \"research\"\n    if not research_dir.is_dir():\n        return []\n\n    completed: list[Path] = []\n    for md_file in sorted(research_dir.glob(\"*.md\")):\n        try:\n            content = md_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n            if len(content.strip()) == 0:\n                continue\n            # Skip files with incomplete markers\n            if re.search(r\"\\[INCOMPLETE\\]\", content, re.IGNORECASE):\n                continue\n            completed.append(md_file)\n        except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError):\n            continue\n    return completed\n\n\ndef discover_synth_files(task_dir: Path) -> list[Path]:\n    \"\"\"Find synthesis files matching the ``synth-*.md`` pattern.\"\"\"\n    synth_dir = task_dir / \"synthesis\"\n    if not synth_dir.is_dir():\n        return []\n    return sorted(synth_dir.glob(\"synth-*.md\"))\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Template Selection (FR-PRD.6)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef select_template(prd_scope: str) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Select the PRD template variant based on scope.\n\n    Returns:\n        2 for \"product\" scope (full creation template)\n        1 for \"feature\" or any other scope (update template)\n    \"\"\"\n    if prd_scope.lower() == \"product\":\n        return 2\n    return 1\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Directory Creation\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef create_task_dirs(task_dir: Path) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Create the 5 required subdirectories for a PRD task.\n\n    Creates: research/, synthesis/, qa/, reviews/, results/\n    \"\"\"\n    for subdir in (\"research\", \"synthesis\", \"qa\", \"reviews\", \"results\"):\n        (task_dir / subdir).mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n```\n\n7. Content of `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/prd/filtering.py`\n\n```python\n\"\"\"PRD pipeline filtering -- partitioning, gap compilation, and synthesis mapping.\n\nPure-function module for splitting file lists into partitions, compiling\ngap reports from research files, merging QA partition reports with\npessimistic verdict strategy, and loading the synthesis mapping table.\n\nDependencies: models (via type hints only)\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport math\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# File Partitioning (FR-PRD.11)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef partition_files(\n    files: list, threshold: int\n) -> list[list]:\n    \"\"\"Split a file list into partitions for parallel processing.\n\n    If the file count is at or below the threshold, returns a single\n    partition containing all files. Above the threshold, splits into\n    roughly even partitions where each partition has at most ``threshold``\n    items.\n\n    Args:\n        files: List of file paths (or any items) to partition.\n        threshold: Maximum items per partition.\n\n    Returns:\n        A list of partitions (each partition is a list of items).\n        Returns an empty list if files is empty.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not files:\n        return []\n    if len(files) <= threshold:\n        return [list(files)]\n\n    num_partitions = math.ceil(len(files) / threshold)\n    partition_size = math.ceil(len(files) / num_partitions)\n\n    partitions: list[list] = []\n    for i in range(0, len(files), partition_size):\n        partitions.append(list(files[i : i + partition_size]))\n    return partitions\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Gap Compilation\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef compile_gaps(research_dir: Path) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Extract and deduplicate gaps from research files.\n\n    Scans all ``.md`` files in the research directory for gap entries.\n    Gaps are identified by lines matching patterns like:\n    - ``- GAP: description``\n    - ``- [ ] Gap: description``\n    - Lines under a ``## Gap Analysis`` or ``## Gaps`` heading\n\n    Returns a deduplicated list of gap dictionaries with keys:\n    ``description``, ``source_file``, ``severity`` (if present).\n    \"\"\"\n    if not research_dir.is_dir():\n        return []\n\n    seen_descriptions: set[str] = set()\n    gaps: list[dict] = []\n\n    for md_file in sorted(research_dir.glob(\"*.md\")):\n        try:\n            content = md_file.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n        except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError):\n            continue\n\n        file_gaps = _extract_gaps_from_content(content, md_file.name)\n        for gap in file_gaps:\n            desc_key = gap[\"description\"].lower().strip()\n            if desc_key not in seen_descriptions:\n                seen_descriptions.add(desc_key)\n                gaps.append(gap)\n\n    return gaps\n\n\ndef _extract_gaps_from_content(content: str, source_file: str) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Extract gap entries from file content.\"\"\"\n    gaps: list[dict] = []\n\n    # Pattern 1: Explicit GAP markers\n    for match in re.finditer(\n        r\"^\\s*[-*]\\s+(?:\\[[ x]\\]\\s+)?(?:GAP|Gap)\\s*:\\s*(.+)$\",\n        content,\n        re.MULTILINE,\n    ):\n        gaps.append({\n            \"description\": match.group(1).strip(),\n            \"source_file\": source_file,\n        })\n\n    # Pattern 2: Items under Gap Analysis heading\n    gap_section = re.search(\n        r\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*#{{1,4}}\\s+(?:Gap\\s+Analysis|Gaps)\\s*\\n(.*?)(?=\\n\\s*#|\\Z)\",\n        content,\n        re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE,\n    )\n    if gap_section:\n        section_text = gap_section.group(1)\n        for line_match in re.finditer(\n            r\"^\\s*[-*]\\s+(.+)$\", section_text, re.MULTILINE\n        ):\n            desc = line_match.group(1).strip()\n            if desc and not desc.startswith(\"#\"):\n                gaps.append({\n                    \"description\": desc,\n                    \"source_file\": source_file,\n                })\n\n    return gaps\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# QA Partition Report Merging (GAP-005)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef merge_qa_partition_reports(reports: list[dict]) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Merge multiple QA partition reports with pessimistic verdict.\n\n    GAP-005: Overall verdict is FAIL if ANY partition reports FAIL.\n    Only returns PASS if ALL partitions pass.\n\n    Args:\n        reports: List of dicts, each with at least a ``verdict`` key\n                 (\"PASS\" or \"FAIL\") and optional ``issues`` list.\n\n    Returns:\n        Merged report dict with ``verdict``, ``partition_count``,\n        ``partitions_passed``, ``partitions_failed``, and ``issues``.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not reports:\n        return {\n            \"verdict\": \"PASS\",\n            \"partition_count\": 0,\n            \"partitions_passed\": 0,\n            \"partitions_failed\": 0,\n            \"issues\": [],\n        }\n\n    all_issues: list[str] = []\n    passed = 0\n    failed = 0\n\n    for report in reports:\n        verdict = report.get(\"verdict\", \"FAIL\").upper()\n        if verdict == \"PASS\":\n            passed += 1\n        else:\n            failed += 1\n        issues = report.get(\"issues\", [])\n        all_issues.extend(issues)\n\n    return {\n        \"verdict\": \"FAIL\" if failed > 0 else \"PASS\",\n        \"partition_count\": len(reports),\n        \"partitions_passed\": passed,\n        \"partitions_failed\": failed,\n        \"issues\": all_issues,\n    }\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# API Contracts\",\n        ],\n        \"source_research\": [\n            \"user flows\",\n            \"per-area research\",\n            \"technical stack\",\n        ],\n    },\n    {\n        \"synth_file\": \"synth-09-resources-maintenance.md\",\n        \"sections\": [\n            \"26. Contributors\",\n            \"27. Related Resources\",\n            \"28. Maintenance & Ownership\",\n        ],\n        \"source_research\": [\n            \"existing docs\",\n            \"all research files\",\n            \"gaps log\",\n        ],\n    },\n]\n\n\ndef load_synthesis_mapping(refs_dir: Path) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Load the synthesis mapping table.\n\n    F-002: Returns exactly 9 entries mapping synthesis files to\n    PRD template sections and their source research inputs.\n\n    Falls back to the built-in default mapping if the refs directory\n    or mapping file is not available.\n\n    Args:\n        refs_dir: Path to the skill refs directory containing\n                  synthesis-mapping.md.\n\n    Returns:\n        List of 9 mapping dicts, each with keys:\n        ``synth_file``, ``sections``, ``source_research``.\n    \"\"\"\n    # The mapping is compiled from the spec; the .md file is the\n    # human-readable reference. We use the built-in default.\n    return list(_DEFAULT_SYNTHESIS_MAPPING)\n\n\ndef _filter_research_for_sections(\n    research_files: list[Path], mapping_entry: dict\n) -> list[Path]:\n    \"\"\"Filter research files relevant to a synthesis section mapping.\n\n    Matches research files to a mapping entry's source_research\n    descriptors using filename heuristics.\n\n    Args:\n        research_files: All available research files.\n        mapping_entry: A single entry from the synthesis mapping table.\n\n    Returns:\n        Subset of research_files relevant to the mapping entry.\n    \"\"\"\n    source_hints = mapping_entry.get(\"source_research\", [])\n\n    # \"all research files\" means return everything\n    for hint in source_hints:\n        if \"all research\" in hint.lower():\n            return list(research_files)\n\n    matched: list[Path] = []\n    for f in research_files:\n        fname = f.stem.lower().replace(\"-\", \" \").replace(\"_\", \" \")\n        for hint in source_hints:\n            # Extract keywords from the hint\n            hint_lower = hint.lower()\n            # Remove parenthetical qualifiers\n            hint_clean = re.sub(r\"\\(.*?\\)\", \"\", hint_lower).strip()\n            keywords = [w for w in hint_clean.split() if len(w) > 2]\n            if any(kw in fname for kw in keywords):\n                matched.append(f)\n                break\n\n    return matched\n```\n\n8. Content of `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/prd/prompts.py`\n\nThis file exists and is substantial. inline file content capped at 50KB.\nNFR-PRD.11/GAP-004: Context injection via ``context_summaries`` parameter.\n\"\"\"\n```\n\nHelpers:\n\n```python\n_TRUNCATION_MARKER = \"\\n\\n[TRUNCATED — file exceeds 50KB inline limit]\"\n\ndef _load_json(path: Path) -> dict:\n    return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\"))\n\ndef _read_file(path: Path, max_bytes: int = 50_000) -> str:\n    content = path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\n    if len(content) > max_bytes:\n        return content[:max_bytes] + _TRUNCATION_MARKER\n    return content\n\ndef _today() -> str:\n    return date.today().isoformat()\n```\n\nNotable exact prompt characteristics:\n- `build_parse_request_prompt` returns strict JSON extraction instructions with fields:\n  `GOAL`, `PRODUCT_NAME`, `PRODUCT_SLUG`, `PRD_SCOPE`, `SCENARIO`, `WHERE`, `WHY`, `TIER_RECOMMENDATION`\n- `build_task_file_prompt` instructs use of Template 02 and says:\n  `CRITICAL: Use incremental file writing. Create the file with frontmatter first, then append each phase section using Edit. Never one-shot the entire file.`\n- `build_investigation_prompt` contains a \"Documentation Staleness Protocol\" with markers:\n  - `[CODE-VERIFIED]`\n  - `[CODE-CONTRADICTED]`\n  - `[UNVERIFIED]`\n- `build_synthesis_prompt` enforces:\n  - template-first reading\n  - evidence-only claims\n  - tables over prose\n  - no full code reproduction\n  - incremental writes\n- `build_assembly_prompt` imports `discover_synth_files` from inventory and assembles final PRD from actual synth files\n- `build_structural_qa_prompt` and `build_qualitative_qa_prompt` both allow in-place fixes\n- `build_gap_filling_prompt` targets a single QA failure for research or synthesis fix cycles\n\nEnd of file:\n\n```python\ndef build_gap_filling_prompt(\n    failure: dict,\n    config: PrdConfig,\n    cycle: int,\n    phase: str,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Fix cycle: Spawn targeted agents to address specific QA failures.\n    ...\n    \"\"\"\n    return f\"\"\"Address this specific QA failure from fix cycle {cycle}.\n\nPhase: {phase}\nFailure:\n  Area: {failure['area']}\n  Issue: {failure['issue']}\n  Severity: {failure['severity']}\n  Remediation: {failure.get('remediation', 'Not specified')}\n\nTask directory: {config.task_dir}\nResearch directory: {config.research_dir}\n\nYour ONLY job is to fix this specific gap. Do not re-do work that already passed QA.\n...\nEXIT_RECOMMENDATION: CONTINUE\n\"\"\"\n```\n\nIf you want, I can next break `prompts.py` into a phase-by-phase implementation map with each builder’s inputs, output files, and downstream dependency edges.\n\n9. Content of `/config/workspace/SuperClaude_Framework/src/superclaude/cli/prd/config.py`\n\n```python\n\"\"\"PRD pipeline configuration -- CLI argument resolution and PrdConfig construction.\n\nResolves CLI arguments into a fully-populated ``PrdConfig`` instance with\nsensible defaults, path resolution, and tier validation.\n\nNFR-PRD.1: Zero ``async def`` or ``await`` in this module.\nNFR-PRD.7: No superclaude.cli.prd.models import PrdConfig\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Constants\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n_VALID_TIERS = frozenset({\"lightweight\", \"standard\", \"heavyweight\"})\n\n# Known step ID patterns for resume validation.\n# Step IDs follow the pattern: word-word or word-word-word (kebab-case).\n_STEP_ID_PATTERN = re.compile(\n    r\"^(check-existing|parse-request|scope-discovery|research-notes\"\n    r\"|sufficiency-review|template-triage|build-task-file|verify-task-file\"\n    r\"|preparation|investigation-\\d+|web-research-\\d+\"\n    r\"|analyst-completeness|qa-research-gate\"\n    r\"|synthesis-\\d+|analyst-synthesis|qa-synthesis-gate\"\n    r\"|assembly|structural-qa|qualitative-qa|completion)$\"\n)\n\n_DEFAULT_SKILL_REFS_DIRS = [\n    Path(\"src/superclaude/skills/prd/refs\"),\n    Path(\".claude/skills/prd/refs\"),\n]\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Public API\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef resolve_config(\n    request: str,\n    *,\n    product: Optional[str] = None,\n    where: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None,\n    output: Optional[str] = None,\n    tier: Optional[str] = None,\n    max_turns: Optional[int] = None,\n    model: Optional[str] = None,\n    dry_run: bool = False,\n    debug: bool = False,\n    resume_from: Optional[str] = None,\n) -> PrdConfig:\n    \"\"\"Resolve CLI arguments into a fully-populated ``PrdConfig``.\n    ...\n    \"\"\"\n    # -- Tier validation --\n    resolved_tier = (tier or \"standard\").lower().strip()\n    if resolved_tier not in _VALID_TIERS:\n        raise ValueError(\n            f\"Invalid tier {resolved_tier!r}. \"\n            f\"Must be one of: {', '.join(sorted(_VALID_TIERS))}\"\n        )\n\n    # -- Resume validation --\n    if resume_from is not None:\n        resume_from = resume_from.strip()\n        if not _STEP_ID_PATTERN.match(resume_from):\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"Unrecognised resume step ID: {resume_from!r}. \"\n                f\"Expected a known step pattern like directory: derived from output_path + product_slug (or 'prd-task')\n    task_dir_name = f\"prd-{product_slug}\" if product_slug else \"prd-task\"\n    task_dir = output_path / task_dir_name\n\n    # Skill refs directory: auto-discover from known locations\n    skill_refs_dir = _discover_skill_refs_dir()\n\n    return PrdConfig(\n        user_message=request,\n        product_name=product_name,\n        product_slug=product_slug,\n        where=list(where) if where else [],\n        output_path=output_path,\n        tier=resolved_tier,\n        task_dir=task_dir,\n        skill_refs_dir=skill_refs_dir,\n        max_turns=max_turns or 300,\n        model=model or \"\",\n        dry_run=dry_run,\n        debug=debug,\n        resume_from=resume_from,\n    )\n\n\ndef _slugify(name: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Convert a product name to a kebab-case slug.\"\"\"\n    slug = name.lower().strip()\n    slug = re.sub(r\"[^a-z0-9]+\", \"-\", slug)\n    return slug.strip(\"-\")\n\n\ndef _discover_skill_refs_dir() -> Path:\n    \"\"\"Find the skill refs directory from known locations.\n\n    Returns the first existing directory, or the default if none found.\n    \"\"\"\n    for candidate in _DEFAULT_SKILL_REFS_DIRS:\n        if candidate.is_dir():\n            return candidate\n    # Fallback to the canonical source location even if it doesn't exist yet\n    return _DEFAULT_SKILL_REFS_DIRS[0]\n```\n\n10. \"\"\"Check that content contains a verdict field set to PASS or FAIL.\n40\t\n41\t    Accepts both JSON format (\"verdict\": \"PASS\") and markdown format\n42\t    (verdict: PASS or **Verdict**: (r\"\\[FILL[^\\]]*\\]\", \"[FILL...]\"),\n69\t    ]\n70\t    found = []\n71\t    for pattern, label in patterns:\n72\t        matches = re.findall(pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE)\n73\t        if )\n161\t    if len(phase_headings) < 2:\n162\t        return f\"Expected multiple phase headings, found {len(phase_headings)}\"\n163\t    return True\n164\t\n165\t\n166\tdef violations in\n170\t    checklist items (lines starting with '- [ ]' or '- [x]').\n171\t    \"\"\"\n172\t    violations = []\n173\t    for match in re.finditer(\n174\t        r\"^\\s*-\\s+\\[[ _check_parallel_instructions(content: str) -> bool | str:\n189\t    \"\"\"Check that phases 2-5 contain parallel execution keywords.\n190\t\n191\t    Validates that later phases include 'parallel', organized in\ntwo layers:\n\n  1. PRD-specific checks: _check_parsed_request_fields,\n     _check_research_notes_sections, _check_suggested_phases_detail,\n     _check_task_phases_present, _check_b2_self_contained,\n     _check_parallel_instructions, _check_prd_template_sections,\n     _check_qa_verdict\n\nAll semantic check functions follow Callable[[str], bool | str] signature:\n  - Return True on pass\n  - Return an error string on failure\n\nAll checks are wrapped in try/except: exceptions return\n(False, \"check '{name}' crashed: {error}\") per F-005.\n\nNFR-PRD.2: All _check_* functions match SemanticCheck\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Layer 1: Reusable semantic checks\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _check_verdict_field(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that content contains a verdict field set to PASS or FAIL.\n\n    Accepts both JSON format (\"verdict\": \"PASS\") and markdown format\n    (verdict: PASS or **Verdict**: PASS).\n    \"\"\"\n    # JSON format\n    json_match = re.search(r'\"verdict\"\\s*:\\s*\"(PASS|FAIL)\"', content)\n    if json_match:\n        return True\n    # Markdown format (case-insensitive key, case-sensitive value)\n    md_match = re.search(\n        r\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*\\*{0,2}[Vv]erdict\\*{0,2}\\s*:\\s*(PASS|FAIL)\",\n        content,\n    )\n    if md_match:\n        return True\n    return \"No verdict field found (expected 'verdict: PASS' or 'verdict: FAIL')\"\n\n\ndef _check_no_placeholders(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that content contains no placeholder text.\n\n    Catches TODO, TBD, PLACEHOLDER, [INSERT], and similar markers.\n    \"\"\"\n    patterns = [\n        (r\"\\bTODO\\b\", \"TODO\"),\n        (r\"\\bTBD\\b\", \"TBD\"),\n        (r\"\\bPLACEHOLDER\\b\", \"PLACEHOLDER\"),\n        (r\"\\[INSERT[^\\]]*\\]\", \"[INSERT...]\"),\n        (r\"\\[FILL[^\\]]*\\]\", \"[FILL...]\"),\n    ]\n    found = []\n    for pattern, label in patterns:\n        matches = re.findall(pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE)\n        if matches:\n            found.append(f\"{label} ({len(matches)}x)\")\n    if found:\n        return f\"Placeholder text found: {', '.join(found)}\"\n    return True\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Layer 2: PRD-specific semantic checks\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _check_parsed_request_fields(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that parsed request contains GOAL, PRODUCT_SLUG, PRD_SCOPE, SCENARIO.\"\"\"\n    required = [\"GOAL\", \"PRODUCT_SLUG\", \"PRD_SCOPE\", \"SCENARIO\"]\n    missing = []\n    for field_name in required:\n        # Check JSON key format\n        json_pat = re.compile(rf'\"{field_name}\"\\s*:\\s*\"[^\"]+\"', re.IGNORECASE)\n        # Check markdown key: value format\n        md_pat = re.compile(\n            rf\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*\\*{{0,2}}{field_name}\\*{{0,2}}\\s*:\\s*\\S\",\n            re.IGNORECASE,\n        )\n        if not json_pat.search(content) and not md_pat.search(content):\n            missing.append(field_name)\n    if missing:\n        return f\"Missing required fields: {', '.join(missing)}\"\n    return True\n\n\n_RESEARCH_REQUIRED_SECTIONS = [\n    \"Product Capabilities\",\n    \"Technical Architecture\",\n    \"User Flows\",\n    \"Integration Points\",\n    \"Existing Documentation\",\n    \"Gap Analysis\",\n    \"Suggested Phases\",\n]\n\n\ndef _check_research_notes_sections(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that research notes contain all 7 required sections.\"\"\"\n    missing = []\n    for section in _RESEARCH_REQUIRED_SECTIONS:\n        # Match as markdown heading (## or ###) or bold text\n        heading_pat = re.compile(\n            rf\"^\\s*#{{1,4}}\\s+.*{re.escape(section)}\", re.MULTILINE | re.IGNORECASE\n        )\n        bold_pat = re.compile(\n            rf\"\\*\\*{re.escape(section)}\\*\\*\", re.IGNORECASE\n        )\n        if not heading_pat.search(content) and not bold_pat.search(content):\n            missing.append(section)\n    if missing:\n        return f\"Missing research sections: {', '.join(missing)}\"\n    return True\n\n\ndef _check_suggested_phases_detail(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that the Suggested Phases section contains per-agent detail.\n\n    Expects at least one numbered or bulleted list item under a Phases heading.\n    \"\"\"\n    phases_match = re.search(\n        r\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*#{1,4}\\s+.*(?:Suggested\\s+)?Phases\",\n        content,\n        re.IGNORECASE,\n    )\n    if not phases_match:\n        return \"No 'Suggested Phases' section found\"\n    # Check for list items after the heading\n    after_heading = content[phases_match.end() :]\n    list_pat = re.search(r\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*(?:\\d+\\.|[-*])\\s+\\S\", after_heading)\n    if not list_pat:\n        return \"Suggested Phases section has no detail items\"\n    return True\n\n\ndef _check_task_phases_present(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that the task file contains phase definitions.\n\n    Expects headings like 'Phase 1:', 'Phase 2:', etc.\n    \"\"\"\n    phase_headings = re.findall(\n        r\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*#{1,4}\\s+.*Phase\\s+\\d\", content, re.IGNORECASE\n    )\n    if len(phase_headings) < 2:\n        return f\"Expected multiple phase headings, found {len(phase_headings)}\"\n    return True\n\n\ndef _check_b2_self_contained(content: str) -> bool | str:\n    \"\"\"Check that checklist items do not reference external content.\n\n    Catches 'see above', 'as mentioned', 'refer to' violations in\n    checklist items (lines starting with '- [ ]' or '- [x]').\n    \"\"\"\n    violations = []\n    for match in re.finditer(\n        r\"^\\s*-\\s+\\[[ x]\\]\\s+(.+)$\", content, re.MULTILINE\n    ):\n        item_text = match.group(1)\n        for phrase in [\"see above\", \"as mentioned\", \"refer to\", \"as described\"]:\n            if phrase.lower() in item_text.lower():\n                violations.append(f\"'{phrase}' in: {item_text[:60]}\")\n    if violations:\n        return (\n            f\"Self-containment violations contain parallel execution keywords.\n\n    Validates that later phases include 'parallel', 'concurrent',\n    'simultaneously', or 'batch' instructions.\n    \"\"\"\n    parallel_keywords = [\"parallel\", \"concurrent\", \"simultaneously\", \"batch\"]\n    # Find phase sections 2+\n    phase_sections = list(\n        re.finditer(\n            r\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*#{1,4}\\s+.*Phase\\s+(\\d+)\",\n            content,\n            re.IGNORECASE,\n        )\n    )\n    later_phases = [m for m in phase_sections if int(m.group(1)) >= 2]\n    if not Partial lines are buffered across poll cycles so no\n    data is lost when a write straddles a poll boundary.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, output_path: Path, poll_interval: float = 0.5):\n        self.output_path = output_path\n        self.poll_interval = poll_interval\n        self.state = MonitorState()\n        self._stop_event = threading.Event()\n        self._thread: threading.Thread | None = None\n        self._last_read_pos: int = 0\n        self._line_buffer: str = \"\"\n        self._seen_files: set[str] = set()\n\n    def start(self):\n        \"\"\"Start the monitor thread.\"\"\"\n        self._stop_event.clear()\n        self._last_read_pos = 0\n        self._line_buffer = \"\"\n        self._seen_files.clear()\n        self.state = MonitorState()\n        self._thread = threading.Thread(\n            target=self._poll_loop,\n            daemon=True,\n            name=\"output-monitor\",\n        )\n        self._thread.start()\n\n    def stop(self):\n        \"\"\"Stop the monitor thread.\"\"\"\n        self._stop_event.set()\n        if self._thread:\n            self._thread.join(timeout=2.0)\n\n    def reset(self, new_output_path: Path):\n        \"\"\"Reset for a new phase (new output file).\"\"\"\n        self.output_path = new_output_path\n        self._last_read_pos = 0\n        self._line_buffer = \"\"\n        self._seen_files.clear()\n        self.state = MonitorState()\n\n    def _poll_loop(self):\n        while not self._stop_event.is_set():\n            self._poll_once()\n            self._stop_event.wait(self.poll_interval)\n\n    def _poll_once(self):\n        now = time.monotonic()\n\n        try:\n            size = self.output_path.stat().st_size\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            return\n\n        self.state.output_bytes_prev = self.state.output_bytes\n        self.state.output_bytes = size\n\n        if size > self._last_read_pos:\n            # New data available — read incremental chunk\n            chunk = self._read_new_chunk(size)\n            if chunk:\n                self._process_chunk(chunk, now)\n        else:\n            # No growth — update stall counter\n            self.state.stall_seconds = now - self.state.last_event_time\n\n        debug_log(\n            _dbg,\n            \"output_file_stat\",\n            path=str(self.output_path),\n            size=size,\n            events_received=self.state.events_received,\n            last_event_time=round(self.state.last_event_time, 1),\n        )\n\n        # Growth rate: exponential moving average\n        delta = self.state.output_bytes - self.state.output_bytes_prev\n        alpha = 0.3\n        self.state.growth_rate_bps = (\n            alpha * (delta / self.poll_interval)\n            + (1 - alpha) * self.state.growth_rate_bps\n        )\n\n    def _read_new_chunk(self, current_size: int) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Read only the bytes added since last poll.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            with open(self.output_path, errors=\"replace\") as f:\n                f.seek(self._last_read_pos)\n                chunk = f.read(current_size - self._last_read_pos)\n                self._last_read_pos = current_size\n                return chunk\n        except (OSError, UnicodeDecodeError):\n            return \"\"\n\n    def _process_chunk(self, chunk: str, now: float):\n        \"\"\"Split chunk into lines, parse complete NDJSON lines, buffer partials.\"\"\"\n        # Process all complete lines (everything except the last split element)\n        for line in lines[:-1]:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line:\n                continue\n\n            # Update liveness on every complete line\n            self.state.last_growth_time = now\n            self.state.last_event_time = now\n            self.state.stall_seconds = 0.0\n            self.state.events_received += 1\n            self.state.lines_total += 1\n\n            # DiagnosticCollector:\n    \"\"\"Collects diagnostic evidence from various sources into a bundle.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, config: SprintConfig):\n        self.config = config\n\n    def collect(\n        self,\n        phase: Phase,\n        phase_result: PhaseResult,\n        monitor_state: MonitorState,\n    ) -> DiagnosticBundle:\n        \"\"\"Collect all available diagnostic data for a failed phase.\n\n        Reads the debug log (if enabled), output and error files, and\n        captures the monitor state snapshot.\n        \"\"\"\n        bundle = DiagnosticBundle(\n            phase=phase,\n            phase_result=phase_result,\n            config=self.config,\n        )\n\n        # Capture monitor state\n        bundle.monitor_state_snapshot = {\n \"stall_status\": monitor_state.stall_status,\n            \"last_task_id\": monitor_state.last_task_id,\n            \"last_tool_used\": monitor_state.last_tool_used,\n            \"files_changed\": entries for this phase\n        if self.config.debug and self.config.debug_log_path.exists():\n            bundle.debug_log_entries = self._read_phase_debug_entries(phase)\n bundle.last_events = bundle.debug_log_entries[-20:]\n\n        # Read output tail\n        output_file = self.config.output_file(phase)\n        if output_file.exists():\n            bundle.output_tail = self._read_tail(output_file, lines=10)\n\n        # Read stderr tail\n        error_file = self.config.error_file(phase)\n        if error_file.exists():\n            bundle.stderr_tail = self._read_tail(error_file, lines=10)\n\n        return bundle\n\n    def _read_phase_debug_entries(self, phase: Phase) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Read debug log entries relevant to this phase.\"\"\"\n        entries = []\n        in_phase = False\n        try:\n            for line in self.config.debug_log_path.open():\n                line = line.rstrip()\n                if not line or line.startswith(\"#\"):\n                    continue\n                if \"PHASE_BEGIN\" in line and f\"phase={phase.number}\" in line:\n                    in_phase = True\n \"\"\"Classifies phase failures based on diagnostic evidence.\"\"\"\n\n    def classify(self, bundle: DiagnosticBundle) -> FailureCategory:\n        \"\"\"Determine the most likely failure category.\n\n        Classification priority:\n        1. STALL: watchdog triggered or high stall duration\n        2. TIMEOUT: exit code 124 (process timeout)\n        3. CRASH: non-zero exit with low stall (subprocess died)\n        4. ERROR: result file indicates error/halt\n        5. UNKNOWN: no clear evidence\n        \"\"\"\n        evidence = []\n        status = bundle.phase_result.status\n        exit_code = bundle.phase_result.exit_code\n\n        # 1. Stall detection\n        if bundle.watchdog_triggered:\n            evidence.append(\"Watchdog triggered — stall detected by timeout\")\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.STALL\n\n        if bundle.stall_duration > 120:\n            evidence.append(f\"High stall duration: {bundle.stall_duration:.0f}s\")\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.STALL\n\n        # 2. Timeout\n        if status == PhaseStatus.TIMEOUT or exit_code == 124:\n            evidence.append(f\"Process timeout (exit code {exit_code})\")\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.TIMEOUT\n\n        # 2.5. Context exhaustion (prompt too long)\n        from .monitor import detect_prompt_too_long\n\n        if bundle.config is not None:\n            output_file = bundle.config.output_file(bundle.phase_result.phase)\n        else:\n            import warnings\n\n            warnings.warn(\n                \"DiagnosticBundle.config=None is deprecated; pass SprintConfig\",\n                DeprecationWarning,\n                stacklevel=2,\n            )\n            output_file = (\n                bundle.phase_result.phase.file.parent.parent\n                / \"results\"\n                / f\"phase-{bundle.phase_result.phase.number}-output.txt\"\n            )\n\n        if exit_code != 0 and detect_prompt_too_long(output_file):\n            evidence.append(f\"Context exhaustion detected (exit code {exit_code})\")\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.CONTEXT_EXHAUSTION\n\n        # 3. Crash (non-zero exit, not timeout, low stall)\n        if exit_code != 0 and bundle.stall_duration < 30:\n            evidence.append(\n                f\"Process crash (exit code {exit_code}, stall {bundle.stall_duration:.0f}s)\"\n            )\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.CRASH\n\n        # 4. Error (result file signals)\n        if status in (PhaseStatus.HALT, PhaseStatus.ERROR):\n            evidence.append(f\"Phase status: {status.value}\")\n            bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n            return FailureCategory.ERROR\n\n        # 5. Unknown\n        evidence.append(\"No clear failure pattern identified\")\n        bundle.classification_evidence = evidence\n        return FailureCategory.UNKNOWN\n\n\nclass ReportGenerator:\n SprintConfig, SprintResult\n\n\nclass SprintLogger:\n    \"\"\"Dual-format sprint logger: JSONL (machine) + Markdown (human).\n\n    Log levels:\n    - DEBUG: JSONL file only\n    - INFO:  JSONL +                f\"{self.config.end_phase or 'last'}\",\n                \"max_turns\": self.config.max_turns,\n                \"model\": self.config.model or \"default\",\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Markdown\n        end = self.config.end_phase or max(p.number for p in self.config.phases)\n        md = [\n            \"# Sprint Execution Log\",\n            \"\",\n              \"duration_seconds\": (finished_at - started_at).total_seconds(),\n                \"exit_code\": exit_code,\n            }\n        )\n\n    def write_phase_result(self, result: \"killpg\"))\n        pgid = os.getpgid(self._process.pid) if use_pgroup else None\n\n        try:\n            if self._on_signal is not None:\n                self._on_signal(self._process.pid, \"SIGTERM\")\n            if use_pgroup and pgid is not None:\n                os.killpg(pgid, signal.SIGTERM)\n            else:\n                self._process.terminate()\n Detects PRD-specific signals: research file\ncompletion, QA verdict extraction, fix cycle counting, synthesis\ncompletion.\n\nNFR-PRD.1: Zero ``async def`` or ``await`` in this module.\nNFR-PRD.7: No imports from superclaude.cli.sprint or superclaude.cli.roadmap.\nGAP-002: No imports from executor.py or tui.py.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport re\nimport time\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nfrom .models import PrdMonitorState\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Signal detection patterns (PRD-specific)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n# Research file creation: research/NN-*.md\n_RESEARCH_FILE_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"research/\\d{2}-[\\w-]+\\.md\")\n\n# Synthesis file creation: synthesis/synth-*.md\n_SYNTH_FILE_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"synthesis/synth-\\d{2}-[\\w-]+\\.md\")\n\n# QA verdict extraction: \"verdict\": \"PASS\" or verdict: PASS\n_QA_VERDICT_JSON_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\"verdict\"\\s*:\\s*\"(PASS|FAIL)\"')\n_QA_VERDICT_MD_PATTERN = re.compile(\n    r\"(?:^|\\n)\\s*\\*{0,2}[Vv]erdict\\*{0,2}\\s*:\\s*(PASS|FAIL)\"\n)\n\n# Fix cycle counter: \"fix_cycle\": N or fix cycle N\n_FIX_CYCLE_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\"fix_cycle\"\\s*:\\s*(\\d+)')\n_FIX_CYCLE_TEXT_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"fix\\s+cycle\\s+(\\d+)\", re.IGNORECASE)\n\n# Agent type detection\n_AGENT_TYPE_PATTERN = re.compile(\n    r'\"agent_type\"\\s*:\\s*\"(research|synthesis|web|qa|assembly)\"'\n)\n\n# Step ID detection\n_STEP_ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r'\"step_id\"\\s*:\\s*\"([^\"]+)\"')\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# PrdMonitor\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass PrdMonitor:\n    \"\"\"Parses NDJSON output lines and maintains PrdMonitorState.\n\n    Designed to be called from the executor's main loop or a sidecar\n    thread. Each call to ``parse_line`` updates the internal state\n    which the TUI reads via ``get_state``.\n\n    No imports from executor.py or tui.py (GAP-002).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._state = PrdMonitorState()\n\n    def parse_line(self, line: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Parse a single NDJSON line and update monitor state.\n\n        Args:\n            line: A single line from subprocess stdout (may be JSON\n                  or plain text).\n        \"\"\"\n        line = line.strip()\n        if not line:\n            return\n\n        # Update liveness on every non-empty line\n        now = time.monotonic()\n        self._state.last_event_time = now\n        self._state.events_received += 1\n        self._state.output_bytes += len(line.encode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\"))\n\n        # Try JSON parse first\n        event: Optional[dict] = None\n        try:\n            event = json.loads(line)\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):\n            pass\n\n        if event is not None:\n            self._extract_from_event(event)\n\n        # Always run text-based signal extraction (covers both JSON\n        # stringified content and plain text lines)\n        self._extract_from_text(line)\n\n    def check_stall(self, threshold_seconds: float) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if the monitor has stalled (no events within threshold).\n\n        NFR-PRD.5: Stall detection comparing last_event_time to now.\n\n        Args:\n            threshold_seconds: Maximum allowed seconds since last event.\n\n        Returns:\n            True if stalled (no events within threshold), False otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        elapsed = time.monotonic() - self._state.last_event_time\n        return elapsed > threshold_seconds\n\n    def get_state(self) -> PrdMonitorState:\n        \"\"\"Return the current monitor state for TUI consumption.\"\"\"\n        return self._state\n\n    def reset(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Reset monitor state for a new step or phase.\"\"\"\n        self._state = PrdMonitorState()\n\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internal extraction\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _extract_from_event(self, event: dict) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Extract signals from a parsed JSON event.\"\"\"\n        # Step ID\n        step_id = event.get(\"step_id\", \"\")\n        if step_id:\n            self._state.last_step_id = step_id\n\n        # Agent type\n        agent_type = event.get(\"agent_type\", \"\")\n        if agent_type:\n            self._state.current_agent_type = agent_type\n\n        # Current artifact\n        artifact = event.get(\"artifact\", \"\") or event.get(\"file\", \"\")\n        if artifact:\n            self._state.current_artifact = artifact\n\n        # QA verdict from structured event\n        verdict = event.get(\"verdict\", \"\")\n        if verdict in (\"PASS\", \"FAIL\"):\n            self._state.qa_verdict = verdict\n\n        # Fix cycle from structured event\n        fix_cycle = event.get(\"fix_cycle\")\n        if isinstance(fix_cycle, int):\n            self._state.fix_cycle_count = max(\n                self._state.fix_cycle_count, fix_cycle\n            )\n\n    def _extract_from_text(self, text: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Extract signals from text using regex patterns.\"\"\"\n        # Research file completion\n        research_matches = _RESEARCH_FILE_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        if research_matches:\n            self._state.research_files_completed += len(research_matches)\n            self._state.current_artifact = research_matches[-1]\n\n        # Synthesis file completion\n        synth_matches = _SYNTH_FILE_PATTERN.findall(text)\n        if synth_matches:\n            self._state.synth_files_completed += len(synth_matches)\n            self._state.current_artifact = synth_matches[-1]\n\n        # QA verdict (JSON format)\n        verdict_match = _QA_VERDICT_JSON_PATTERN.search(text)\n        if verdict_match:\n            self._state.qa_verdict = verdict_match.group(1)\n\n        # QA verdict (Markdown format)\n        if not verdict_match:\n            md_match = _QA_VERDICT_MD_PATTERN.search(text)\n            if md_match:\n                self._state.qa_verdict = md_match.group(1)\n\n        # Fix cycle counter (JSON)\n        cycle_match = _FIX_CYCLE_PATTERN.search(text)\n        if cycle_match:\n            cycle_num = int(cycle_match.group(1))\n            self._state.fix_cycle_count = max(\n                self._state.fix_cycle_count, cycle_num\n            )\n\n        # Fix cycle counter (text)\n        if not cycle_match:\n            text_cycle = _FIX_CYCLE_TEXT_PATTERN.search(text)\n            if text_cycle:\n                cycle_num = int(text_cycle.group(1))\n                self._state.fix_cycle_count = max(\n                    self._state.fix_cycle_count, cycle_num\n                )\n\n        # Agent type\n        agent_match = _AGENT_TYPE_PATTERN.search(text)\n        if agent_match:\n            self._state.current_agent_type = agent_match.group(1)\n\n        # Step ID\n        step_match = _STEP_ID_PATTERN.search(text)\n        if step_match:\n            self._state.last_step_id = step_match.group(1)\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":563,"cache_read_input_tokens":108531,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":563,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available","speed":"standard"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"553e92c9-be50-45a8-ab06-f448f4f5698c","uuid":"8c83b4b0-feca-445b-bf98-3010541b6ca4"}
