generic error, SIGKILL\n_TRANSIENT_PATTERNS = (\n    \"rate limit\",\n    \"rate_limit\",\n    \"429\",\n    \"503\",\n    \"502\",\n    \"connection refused\",\n    \"connection reset\",\n    \"timeout\",\n    \"ETIMEDOUT\",\n    \"ECONNRESET\",\n    \"temporarily unavailable\",\n)\n\n# Non-transient patterns that should NOT be retried\n_NON_TRANSIENT_PATTERNS = (\n    \"permission denied\",\n    \"invalid arg\",\n    \"invalid option\",\n    \"not found\",\n    \"no such file\",\n    \"usage:\",\n)\n\n\ndef _is_transient_failure(exit_code: int, stderr_text: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Determine if a failure is transient and retryable.\n\n    Returns True for rate limiting, API unavailability, and network\n    errors. Returns False for invalid args, permission denied, etc.\n    \"\"\"\n    stderr_lower = stderr_text.lower()\n\n    # Non-transient patterns always fail immediately\n    for pattern in _NON_TRANSIENT_PATTERNS:\n        if pattern in stderr_lower:\n            return False\n\n    # Check for transient patterns\n    for pattern in _TRANSIENT_PATTERNS:\n        if pattern in stderr_lower:\n            return True\n\n    # Exit code 124 is timeout -- not transient (already timed out)\n    if exit_code == 124:\n        return False\n\n    # Generic non-zero with no matching pattern: not transient\n    return False\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Phase-to-allowed-refs mapping (GAP-003)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n# Maps step IDs to the list of refs files each phase is allowed to read.\n# Files > 50KB are passed as --file args; files < 50KB are inlined in prompt.\n_PHASE_ALLOWED_REFS: dict[str, list[str]] = {\n    \"parse-request\": [],\n    \"scope-discovery\": [],\n    \"research-notes\": [],\n    \"sufficiency-review\": [],\n    \"template-triage\": [\"build-request-template.md\"],\n    \"build-task-file\": [\"build-request-template.md\", \"operational-guidance.md\"],\n    \"verify-task-file\": [\"validation-checklists.md\"],\n    \"preparation\": [\"operational-guidance.md\"],\n    \"investigation\": [\"operational-guidance.md\"],\n    \"research-qa\": [\"validation-checklists.md\"],\n    \"web-research\": [\"operational-guidance.md\"],\n    \"synthesis\": [\"synthesis-mapping.md\", \"operational-guidance.md\"],\n    \"synthesis-qa\": [\"validation-checklists.md\"],\n    \"assembly\": [\"synthesis-mapping.md\", \"operational-guidance.md\"],\n    \"structural-qa\": [\"validation-checklists.md\"],\n    \"qualitative-qa\": [\"validation-checklists.md\"],\n    \"present-complete\": [],\n}\n\n_FILE_SIZE_THRESHOLD = 50_000  # 50KB: inline vs --file cutoff\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# PrdClaudeProcess\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass PrdClaudeProcess(ClaudeProcess):\n    \"\"\"PRD-specific subprocess extending the pipeline base ClaudeProcess.\n\n    Adds:\n    - Phase-aware ``--file`` arg construction (GAP-003)\n    - Subprocess timeout enforcement via Popen watchdog (NFR-PRD.13/F-004)\n    - Launch retry with exponential backoff (NFR-PRD.12/GAP-011)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        config: PrdConfig,\n        step_id: str,\n        prompt: str,\n        output_file: Path,\n        error_file: Path,\n        timeout_seconds: int = 3600,\n        max_retries: int = 2,\n    ) -> None:\n        self.prd_config = config\n        self.step_id = step_id\n        self._max_retries = max_retries\n        self._retry_delays = [5.0, 15.0]  # exponential backoff\n\n        # Build --file args from phase-allowed refs\n        file_args = self._build_file_args(config, step_id)\n\n        super().__init__(\n            prompt=prompt,\n            output_file=output_file,\n            error_file=error_file,\n            max_turns=config.max_turns,\n            model=config.model,\n            permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n            timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,\n            output_format=\"stream-json\",\n            extra_args=file_args,\n        )\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _build_file_args(config: PrdConfig, step_id: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Build --file args for refs files allowed by this step.\n\n        GAP-003: Each subprocess receives only refs files permitted\n        for its phase. Files > 50KB are passed as --file args;\n        files < 50KB would be inlined in the prompt by the prompt\n        builder (not handled here).\n        \"\"\"\n        # Normalize step_id: \"investigation-3\" -> \"investigation\"\n        base_step = step_id.rsplit(\"-\", 1)[0] if step_id[-1:].isdigit() else step_id\n\n        allowed = _PHASE_ALLOWED_REFS.get(base_step, [])\n        if not allowed:\n            return []\n\n        file_args: list[str] = []\n        for ref_name in allowed:\n            ref_path = config.skill_refs_dir / ref_name\n            if ref_path.is_file():\n                try:\n                    size = ref_path.stat().st_size\n                except OSError:\n                    continue\n                if size > _FILE_SIZE_THRESHOLD:\n                    file_args.extend([\"--file\", str(ref_path)])\n\n        return file_args\n\n    def start_with_retry(self) -> subprocess.Popen:\n        \"\"\"Launch the process with retry on transient failures.\n\n        NFR-PRD.12/GAP-011: Retry up to 2 times with exponential\n        backoff (5s, 15s) on transient failures. Non-transient\n        failures (invalid args, permission denied) fail immediately.\n\n        Returns:\n            The Popen process on success.\n\n        Raises:\n            RuntimeError: If all retries are exhausted or a\n                non-transient failure occurs.\n        \"\"\"\n        last_error: Optional[Exception] = None\n\n        for attempt in range(self._max_retries + 1):\n            try:\n                proc = self.start()\n                return proc\n            except OSError as exc:\n                last_error = exc\n                stderr_text = str(exc)\n\n                if not _is_transient_failure(1, stderr_text):\n                    raise RuntimeError(\n                        f\"Non-transient launch failure for step \"\n                        f\"{self.step_id!r}: {exc}\"\n                    ) from exc\n\n                if attempt < self._max_retries:\n                    delay = self._retry_delays[attempt]\n                    _log.warning(\n                        \"Transient failure on attempt %d for step %s, \"\n                        \"retrying in %.0fs: %s\",\n                        attempt + 1,\n                        self.step_id,\n                        delay,\n                        exc,\n                    )\n                    time.sleep(delay)\n\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"All {self._max_retries + 1} launch attempts failed for \"\n            f\"step {self.step_id!r}: {last_error}\"\n        )\n\n    def terminate(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Graceful shutdown: SIGTERM -> 5s grace -> SIGKILL.\n\n        NFR-PRD.13/F-004: Override base terminate to use 5s grace\n        period instead of default 10s.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self._process is None or self._process.poll() is not None:\n            self._close_handles()\n            return\n\n        use_pgroup = all(hasattr(os, attr) for attr in (\"getpgid\", \"killpg\"))\n        pgid = os.getpgid(self._process.pid) if use_pgroup else None\n\n        try:\n            if self._on_signal is not None:\n                self._on_signal(self._process.pid, \"SIGTERM\")\n            if use_pgroup and pgid is not None:\n                os.killpg(pgid, signal.SIGTERM)\n            else:\n                self._process.terminate()\n            _log.debug(\"SIGTERM sent to pid=%d\", self._process.pid)\n        except ProcessLookupError:\n            self._close_handles()\n            return\n\n        try:\n            self._process.wait(timeout=5)  # 5s grace per F-004\n        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n            try:\n                if use_pgroup and pgid is not None:\n                    os.killpg(pgid, signal.SIGKILL)\n                else:\n                    self._process.kill()\n                _log.debug(\"SIGKILL sent to pid=%d\", self._process.pid)\n                self._process.wait(timeout=5)\n            except (ProcessLookupError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired):\n                pass\n\n        if self._on_exit is not None:\n            self._on_exit(self._process.pid, self._process.returncode)\n        self._close_handles()\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":542,"cache_read_input_tokens":111918,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":542,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available","speed":"standard"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"553e92c9-be50-45a8-ab06-f448f4f5698c","uuid":"98f1e467-e138-43fb-be78-677dda3f9d6b"}

Returns False for invalid args, permission denied, etc.\n    \"\"\"\n    stderr_lower = stderr_text.lower()\n\n    # Non-transient patterns always fail immediately\n    for pattern in _NON_TRANSIENT_PATTERNS:\n        if pattern in stderr_lower:\n            return False\n\n    # Generic non-zero with no matching pattern: not transient\n    return False\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Phase-to-allowed-refs mapping (GAP-003)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n# Maps step IDs to the list of refs files each phase is allowed to read.\n# Files > 50KB are passed as --file args; files < 50KB are inlined in prompt.\n_PHASE_ALLOWED_REFS: dict[str, list[str]] = {\n    \"parse-request\": [],\n    \"scope-discovery\": [],\n    \"research-notes\": [],\n    \"sufficiency-review\": [],\n    \"template-triage\": [\"build-request-template.md\"],\n    \"build-task-file\": [\"build-request-template.md\", \"operational-guidance.md\"],\n    \"verify-task-file\": [\"validation-checklists.md\"],\n    \"preparation\": [\"operational-guidance.md\"],\n    \"investigation\": [\"operational-guidance.md\"],\n    \"research-qa\": [\"validation-checklists.md\"],\n    \"web-research\": [\"operational-guidance.md\"],\n    \"synthesis\": [\"synthesis-mapping.md\", \"operational-guidance.md\"],\n    \"synthesis-qa\": [\"validation-checklists.md\"],\n    \"assembly\": [\"synthesis-mapping.md\", \"operational-guidance.md\"],\n    \"structural-qa\": [\"validation-checklists.md\"],\n    \"qualitative-qa\": [\"validation-checklists.md\"],\n    \"present-complete\": [],\n}\n\n_FILE_SIZE_THRESHOLD = 50_000  # 50KB: inline vs --file cutoff\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# PrdClaudeProcess\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass PrdClaudeProcess(ClaudeProcess):\n    \"\"\"PRD-specific subprocess extending the pipeline base ClaudeProcess.\n\n    Adds:\n    - Phase-aware ``--file`` arg construction (GAP-003)\n    - Subprocess timeout enforcement via Popen watchdog (NFR-PRD.13/F-004)\n    - Launch retry with exponential backoff (NFR-PRD.12/GAP-011)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        *,\n        config: PrdConfig,\n        step_id: str,\n        prompt: str,\n        output_file: Path,\n        error_file: Path,\n        timeout_seconds: int = 3600,\n        max_retries: int = 2,\n    ) -> None:\n        self.prd_config = config\n        self.step_id = step_id\n        self._max_retries = max_retries\n        self._retry_delays = [5.0, 15.0]  # exponential backoff\n\n        # Build --file args from phase-allowed refs\n        file_args = self._build_file_args(config, step_id)\n\n        super().__init__(\n            prompt=prompt,\n            output_file=output_file,\n            error_file=error_file,\n            max_turns=config.max_turns,\n            model=config.model,\n            permission_flag=config.permission_flag,\n            timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,\n            output_format=\"stream-json\",\n            extra_args=file_args,\n        )\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _build_file_args(config: PrdConfig, step_id: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Build --file args for refs files allowed by this step.\n\n        GAP-003: Each subprocess receives only refs files permitted\n        for its phase. Files > 50KB are passed as --file args;\n        files < 50KB would be inlined in the prompt by the prompt\n        builder (not handled here).\n        \"\"\"\n        # Normalize step_id: \"investigation-3\" -> \"investigation\"\n        base_step = step_id.rsplit(\"-\", 1)[0] if step_id[-1:].isdigit() else step_id\n\n        allowed = _PHASE_ALLOWED_REFS.get(base_step, [])\n        if not allowed:\n            return []\n\n        file_args: list[str] = []\n        for ref_name in allowed:\n            ref_path = config.skill_refs_dir / ref_name\n            if ref_path.is_file():\n                try:\n                    size = ref_path.stat().st_size\n                except OSError:\n                    continue\n                if size > _FILE_SIZE_THRESHOLD:\n                    file_args.extend([\"--file\", str(ref_path)])\n\n        return file_args\n\n    def start_with_retry(self) -> subprocess.Popen:\n        \"\"\"Launch the process with retry on transient failures.\n\n        NFR-PRD.12/GAP-011: Retry up to 2 times with exponential\n        backoff (5s, 15s) on transient failures. Non-transient\n        failures (invalid args, permission denied) fail immediately.\n\n        Returns:\n            The Popen process on success.\n\n        Raises:\n            RuntimeError: If all retries are exhausted or a\n                non-transient failure occurs.\n        \"\"\"\n        last_error: Optional[Exception] = None\n\n        for attempt in range(self._max_retries + 1):\n            try:\n                proc = self.start()\n                return proc\n            except OSError as exc:\n                last_error = exc\n                stderr_text = str(exc)\n\n                if not _is_transient_failure(1, stderr_text):\n                    raise RuntimeError(\n                        f\"Non-transient launch failure for step \"\n                        f\"{self.step_id!r}: {exc}\"\n                    ) from exc\n\n                if attempt < self._max_retries:\n                    delay = self._retry_delays[attempt]\n                    _log.warning(\n                        \"Transient failure on attempt %d for step %s, \"\n                        \"retrying in %.0fs: %s\",\n                        attempt + 1,\n                        self.step_id,\n                        delay,\n                        exc,\n                    )\n                    time.sleep(delay)\n\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"All {self._max_retries + 1} launch attempts failed for \"\n            f\"step {self.step_id!r}: {last_error}\"\n        )\n\n    def terminate(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Graceful shutdown: SIGTERM -> 5s grace -> SIGKILL.\n\n        NFR-PRD.13/F-004: Override base terminate to use 5s grace\n        period instead of default 10s.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self._process is None or self._process.poll() is not None:\n            self._close_handles()\n            return\n\n        use_pgroup = all(hasattr(os, attr) for attr in (\"getpgid\", \"killpg\"))\n        pgid = os.getpgid(self._process.pid) if use_pgroup else None\n\n        try:\n            if self._on_signal is not None:\n                self._on_signal(self._process.pid, \"SIGTERM\")\n            if use_pgroup and pgid is not None:\n                os.killpg(pgid, signal.SIGTERM)\n            else:\n                self._process.terminate()\n            _log.debug(\"SIGTERM sent to pid=%d\", self._process.pid)\n        except ProcessLookupError:\n            self._close_handles()\n            return\n\n        try:\n            self._process.wait(timeout=5)  # 5s grace per F-004\n        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:\n            try:\n                if use_pgroup and pgid is not None:\n                    os.killpg(pgid, signal.SIGKILL)\n                else:\n                    self._process.kill()\n                _log.debug(\"SIGKILL sent to pid=%d\", self._process.pid)\n                self._process.wait(timeout=5)\n            except (ProcessLookupError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired):\n                pass\n\n        if self._on_exit is not None:\n            self._on_exit(self._process.pid, self._process.returncode)\n        self._close_handles()\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

Markdown entry (inline note)\n        self._write_md(\n            f\"| {step_id} | RUNNING | - | Started: {step_name} |\\n\"\n        )\n\n    def log_step_complete(\n        self,\n        step_id: str,\n        result: str,\n        *,\n        duration_seconds: float = 0.0,\n        exit_code: int = 0,\n        details: str = \"\",\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Log the completion of a pipeline step.\n\n        Args:\n            step_id: Unique step identifier.\n            result: Status string (e.g. \"pass\", \"halt\", \"qa_fail\").\n            duration_seconds: Wall-clock duration of the step.\n            exit_code: Subprocess exit code.\n            details: Additional context string.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # JSONL entry\n        self._write_jsonl({\n            \"timestamp\": now.isoformat(),\n            \"event_type\": \"step_complete\",\n            \"step_id\": step_id,\n            \"duration_seconds\": round(duration_seconds, 2),\n            \"exit_code\": exit_code,\n            \"result\": result,\n            \"details\": details,\n        })\n\n        # Markdown entry\n        status_display = _STATUS_EMOJI.get(result, result.upper())\n        duration_display = self._format_duration(duration_seconds)\n        detail_text = details or f\"exit={exit_code}\"\n        self._write_md(\n            f\"| {step_id} | {status_display} | {duration_display} \"\n            f\"| {detail_text} |\\n\"\n        )\n\n    def log_gate_result(\n        self,\n        step_id: str,\n        passed: bool,\n        message: str,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Log a gate evaluation result.\n\n        Args:\n            step_id: Step the gate applies to.\n            passed: Whether the gate passed.\n            message: Description of gate result.\n        \"\"\"\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # JSONL entry\n        self._write_jsonl({\n            \"timestamp\": now.isoformat(),\n            \"event_type\": \"gate_result\",\n            \"step_id\": step_id,\n            \"passed\": passed,\n            \"message\": message,\n        })\n\n        # Markdown entry\n        gate_status = \"GATE PASS\" if passed else \"GATE FAIL\"\n        self._write_md(\n            f\"| {step_id} | {gate_status} | - | {message} |\\n\"\n        )\n\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internal helpers\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _write_jsonl(self, data: dict) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Append a JSON line to the JSONL log file.\"\"\"\n        with open(self._jsonl_path, \"a\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n            f.write(json.dumps(data, default=str) + \"\\n\")\n\n    def _write_md(self, line: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Append a line to the Markdown log file.\"\"\"\n        with open(self._md_path, \"a\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n            f.write(line)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _format_duration(seconds: float) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format duration as human-readable string.\"\"\"\n        if seconds < 60:\n            return f\"{seconds:.1f}s\"\n        minutes = int(seconds // 60)\n        secs = seconds % 60\n        return f\"{minutes}m{secs:.0f}s\"\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

return report\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# FailureClassifier\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass FailureClassifier:\n    \"\"\"Maps PrdStepStatus values to user-facing failure categories.\"\"\"\n\n    def classify(self, result: PrdStepResult) -> PrdFailureCategory:\n        \"\"\"Determine the failure category for a step result.\n\n        Classification priority:\n        1. TIMEOUT: Step timed out\n        2. QA_FAIL: QA check returned FAIL\n        3. BUDGET_EXHAUSTED: Fix cycles exhausted\n        4. GATE_FAIL: Gate validation failed\n        5. CRASH: Unexpected non-zero exit\n        6. UNKNOWN: No clear evidence\n        \"\"\"\n        status = result.status\n\n        if status == PrdStepStatus.TIMEOUT:\n            return PrdFailureCategory.TIMEOUT\n\n        if status == PrdStepStatus.QA_FAIL:\n            return PrdFailureCategory.QA_FAIL\n\n        if status == PrdStepStatus.QA_FAIL_EXHAUSTED:\n            return PrdFailureCategory.BUDGET_EXHAUSTED\n\n        if status == PrdStepStatus.VALIDATION_FAIL:\n            return PrdFailureCategory.GATE_FAIL\n\n        if status == PrdStepStatus.ERROR:\n            return PrdFailureCategory.CRASH\n\n        if status == PrdStepStatus.HALT:\n            # HALT could be gate failure or crash depending on context\n            if result.gate_failure_reason:\n                return PrdFailureCategory.GATE_FAIL\n ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass ReportGenerator:\n    \"\"\"Generates human-readable diagnostic reports with resume commands.\"\"\"\n\n    def self._step_results\n            if r.status == PrdStepStatus.SKIPPED\n        )\n\n        # Collect failure details\n        report.failure_details = [\n            {\n                \"step_id\": getattr(r.step, \"name\", \"\") if r.step else \"\",\n                \"status\": r.status.value,\n                \"exit_code\": r.exit_code,\n                \"agent_type\": r.agent_type,\n                \"fix_cycle\": r.fix_cycle,\n            }\n            for r in self._step_results\n            if r.status.is_failure\n        ]\n\n        report.gate_failures = list(self._gate_failures)\n        report.fix_cycle_history = list(self._fix_cycle_history)\n        report.resume_command = pipeline_result.resume_command()\n        report.overall_status = pipeline_result.outcome\n\n        return report\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# FailureClassifier\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass FailureClassifier:\n    \"\"\"Maps PrdStepStatus values to user-facing failure categories.\"\"\"\n\n    def classify(self, result: PrdStepResult) -> PrdFailureCategory:\n        \"\"\"Determine the failure category for a step result.\n\n        Classification priority:\n        1. TIMEOUT: Step timed out\n        2. QA_FAIL: QA check returned FAIL\n        3. BUDGET_EXHAUSTED: Fix cycles exhausted\n        4. GATE_FAIL: Gate validation failed\n        5. CRASH: Unexpected non-zero exit\n        6. UNKNOWN: No clear evidence\n        \"\"\"\n        status = result.status\n\n        if status == PrdStepStatus.TIMEOUT:\n            return PrdFailureCategory.TIMEOUT\n\n        if status == PrdStepStatus.QA_FAIL:\n            return PrdFailureCategory.QA_FAIL\n\n        if status == PrdStepStatus.QA_FAIL_EXHAUSTED:\n            return PrdFailureCategory.BUDGET_EXHAUSTED\n\n        if status == PrdStepStatus.VALIDATION_FAIL:\n            return PrdFailureCategory.GATE_FAIL\n\n        if status == PrdStepStatus.ERROR:\n            return PrdFailureCategory.CRASH\n\n        if status == PrdStepStatus.HALT:\n            # HALT could be gate failure or crash depending on context\n            if result.gate_failure_reason:\n                return PrdFailureCategory.GATE_FAIL\n            return PrdFailureCategory.CRASH\n\n        return PrdFailureCategory.UNKNOWN\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# ReportGenerator\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass ReportGenerator:\n    \"\"\"Generates human-readable diagnostic reports with resume commands.\"\"\"\n\n    def generate(self, report: PrdDiagnosticReport) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Produce a diagnostic summary as markdown.\"\"\"\n        lines = [\n            \"# PRD Pipeline Diagnostic Report\",\n            \"\",\n            \"## Summary\",\n            f\"- **Status**: {report.overall_status}\",\n            f\"- **Total Steps**: {report.total_steps}\",\n            f\"- **Passed**: {report.steps_passed}\",\n            f\"- **Failed**: {report.steps_failed}\",\n            f\"- **Skipped**: {report.steps_skipped}\",\n        ]\n\n        if report.failure_details:\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(\"## Failures\")\n            for detail in report.failure_details:\n                step = detail.get(\"step_id\", \"unknown\")\n                status = detail.get(\"status\", \"unknown\")\n                exit_code = detail.get(\"exit_code\", -1)\n                agent = detail.get(\"agent_type\", \"\")\n                cycle = detail.get(\"fix_cycle\", 0)\n                lines.append(\n                    f\"- **{step}**: {status} (exit={exit_code}\"\n                    f\"{f', agent={agent}' if agent else ''}\"\n                    f\"{f', cycle={cycle}' if cycle else ''})\"\n                )\n\n        if report.gate_failures:\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(\"## Gate Failures\")\n            for gf in report.gate_failures:\n                lines.append(\n                    f\"- **{gf['step_id']}** [{gf['enforcement']}]: \"\n                    f\"{gf['reason']}\"\n                )\n\n        if report.fix_cycle_history:\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(\"## Fix Cycle History\")\n            for fc in report.fix_cycle_history:\n                agent_info = f\" (agent: {fc['agent_id']})\" if fc.get(\"agent_id\") else \"\"\n                lines.append(\n                    f\"- **{fc['step_id']}** cycle {fc['cycle_number']}: \"\n                    f\"{fc['verdict']}{agent_info}\"\n                )\n\n        if report.resume_command:\n            lines.append(\"\")\n            lines.append(\"## Resume\")\n            lines.append(f\"```\\n{report.resume_command}\\n```\")\n            suggested_budget = report.config.max_turns\n            lines.append(f\"Suggested budget: {suggested_budget} turns\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n    def generate_resume_command(\n        self,\n        pipeline_result: PrdPipelineResult,\n        *,\n        failed_agent_id: str = \"\",\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a CLI resume command for mid-run failures.\n\n        NFR-PRD.6/GAP-007: Step-level and per-agent granularity.\n\n        Args:\n            pipeline_result: The pipeline result with halt info.\n            failed_agent_id: If the failure occurred in a parallel group,\n                the specific agent that failed (GAP-007).\n\n        Returns:\n            CLI command string for resuming the pipeline.\n        \"\"\"\n        cmd = pipeline_result.resume_command()\n        if not cmd:\n            return \"\"\n\n        # Add per-agent resume hint for parallel group failures\n        if failed_agent_id:\n            cmd += f\" --agent {failed_agent_id}\"\n\n        # Add budget suggestion\n        budget = pipeline_result.suggested_resume_budget\n        cmd += f\" --max-turns {budget}\"\n\n        return cmd\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

              )\n\n    def _render(self) -> Panel:\n        \"\"\"Build the complete TUI layout.\"\"\"\n        table = self._build_step_table()\n        progress = self._build_progress()\n      table.add_column(\"Duration\", width=10, justify=\"right\")\n        table.add_column(\"Agents\", width=7, justify=\"center\")\n        table.add_column(\"QA\", width=10, justify=\"center\")\n\n _GATE_ICONS.get(step.gate_state, \"[dim]--[/]\")\n            qa_display = step.qa_verdict or \"-\"\n            agent_display = str(step.agent_count) if step.agent_count else \"-\"\n\n            # pipeline executor -- main orchestration loop.\n\nImplements the PrdExecutor class that drives the 15-step PRD pipeline:\nsequential dispatch for Stage A (Steps 1-9), dynamic step generation\nfor Stage B (Steps 10-14), and final Step 15.\n\nIntegrates all Phase 1 (models, gates, inventory, filtering), Phase 2\n(prompts, config), and Phase 3 (monitor, process, logging_, diagnostics,\ntui) modules.\n\nNFR-PRD.1: Zero ``async def`` or ``await`` in this module.\nNFR-PRD.3/F-007: Sentinel detection with ``^EXIT_RECOMMENDATION:``\n    anchored regex, skipping code blocks.\nNFR-PRD.4: TurnLedger budget guards before every subprocess launch.\nNFR-PRD.7: No imports from superclaude.cli.sprint or superclaude.cli.roadmap.\nNFR-PRD.9: Signal-aware shutdown with state preservation.\nNFR-PRD.11/GAP-004: Context injection for dependent steps.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport re\nimport signal\nimport time\nfrom concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Callable, Optional\n\nfrom .config import resolve_config\nfrom .diagnostics import (\n    DiagnosticCollector,\n    FailureClassifier,\n    ReportGenerator,\n)\nfrom .filtering import (\n    compile_gaps,\n    load_synthesis_mapping,\n PrdStepResult,\n    PrdStepStatus,\n)\nfrom .monitor import PrdMonitor\nfrom .process import PrdClaudeProcess\nfrom .tui import PrdTUI\n\n# Prompt builders -- imported lazily in _get_prompt_builder to avoid\n# pulling all 19 builders into the module namespace upfront.\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Sentinel detection (NFR-PRD.3 / F-007)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n# Anchored regex: must start at line beginning\n_EXIT_SENTINEL_PATTERN = re.compile(\n    r\"^EXIT_RECOMMENDATION:\\s*(CONTINUE|HALT)\", re.MULTILINE\n)\n\n# Fenced code block detection: ```...```\n_CODE_BLOCK_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"```[\\s\\S]*?```\")\n\n\ndef _detect_sentinel(output: str) -> Optional[str]:\n    \"\"\"Extract EXIT_RECOMMENDATION from subprocess output.\n\n    F-007: Uses ``^EXIT_RECOMMENDATION:`` anchored regex with\n    ``re.MULTILINE``, skipping matches inside fenced code blocks.\n\n    Returns:\n        \"CONTINUE\" or \"HALT\" if a valid sentinel is found outside\n        code blocks, None otherwise.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Remove fenced code blocks before searching\n    cleaned = _CODE_BLOCK_PATTERN.sub(\"\", output)\n\n    match = _EXIT_SENTINEL_PATTERN.search(cleaned)\n    if match:\n        return match.group(1)\n    return None\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Turn ledger (NFR-PRD.4)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass TurnLedger:\n    \"\"\"Budget tracker for subprocess turn allocation.\n\n    Guards against budget exhaustion by tracking allocated and consumed\n    turns across all subprocess launches.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    total_budget: int = 300\n    allocated: int = 0\n    consumed: int = 0\n\n    def can_allocate(self, turns: int) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if the requested turns can be allocated.\"\"\"\n        return (self.allocated + turns) <= self.total_budget\n\n    def allocate(self, turns: int) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Allocate turns from the budget. Returns False if insufficient.\"\"\"\n        if not self.can_allocate(turns):\n            return False\n        self.allocated += turns\n        return True\n\n    def consume(self, turns: int) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Record turns actually consumed by a subprocess.\"\"\"\n        self.consumed += turns\n\n    @property\n    def remaining(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Turns remaining in the budget.\"\"\"\n        return max(self.total_budget - self.allocated, 0)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Signal handler (NFR-PRD.9)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass PrdSignalHandler:\n    \"\"\"Register signal handlers for graceful PRD pipeline shutdown.\n\n    On SIGINT/SIGTERM: set the shutdown flag so the executor loop\n    can write resume state and exit gracefully.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self.shutdown_requested = False\n        self._original_sigint = None\n        self._original_sigterm = None\n\n    def install(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Install signal handlers.\"\"\"\n        self._original_sigint = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGINT)\n        self._original_sigterm = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)\n        signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._handle)\n        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self._handle)\n\n    def uninstall(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Restore original signal handlers.\"\"\"\n        if self._original_sigint is not None:\n            signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._original_sigint)\n        if self._original_sigterm is not None:\n            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self._original_sigterm)\n\n    def _handle(self, signum, frame) -> None:\n        self.shutdown_requested = True\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Step definitions\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# PrdExecutor\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass PrdExecutor:\n    \"\"\"Main PRD pipeline executor.\n\n    Drives the 15-step pipeline through sequential Stage A, dynamic\n    parallel Stage B, and final completion step.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, config: PrdPipelineResult:\n        \"\"\"Execute the full PRD pipeline.\n\n        Returns:\n            PrdPipelineResult with all step outcomes.\n        \"\"\"\n        result = PrdPipelineResult(config=self._config)\n\n        # Dry-run mode: validate config only\n        if self._config.dry_run:\n            result.outcome = \"dry_run\"\n            result.finished_at =           create_task_dirs(self._config.task_dir)\n\n            # Register steps in TUI\n            all_step_ids = [\n                (s[0], s[1]) for s in _STAGE_A_STEPS\n            ]\n            self._tui.register_steps(all_step_ids)\n            self._tui.start()\n\n            # Stage A: Sequential steps 1-9\n            for step_id, step_name, builder_name, _ in _STAGE_A_STEPS:\n                if self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    self._handle_shutdown(result)\n                    return result\n\n                step_result = self._execute_step(\n                    step_id, step_name, builder_name\n                )\n                self._step_results.append(step_result)\n                result.step_results.append(step_result)\n\n                # STRICT gate failure halts pipeline\n                if step_result.status.is_failure:\n                    gate = GATE_CRITERIA.get(step_id)\n                    if gate and gate.enforcement_tier == \"STRICT\":\n                        result.outcome = \"halt\"\n                        result.halt_step = step_id\n                        result.halt_reason = (\n                       \"build_completion_prompt\",\n                    )\n                    self._step_results.append(completion_result)\n -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Step execution\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _execute_step(\n        self,\n        step_id: str,\n        step_name: str,\n        builder_name: str,\n    ) -> PrdStepResult:\n        \"\"\"Execute a single pipeline step.\"\"\"\n        self._logger.log_step_start(step_id, step_name)\n        self._tui.update_step(\n            step_id, status=PrdStepStatus.RUNNING, gate_state=\"PENDING\"\n        )\n\n        start_time =    step_result = self._run_check_existing()\n        elif builder_name == \"_template_triage\":\n            step_result = self._run_template_triage()\n        else:\n            step_result = self._logger.log_step_complete(\n            step_id,\n            step_result.status.value,\n            duration_seconds=duration,\n            exit_code=step_result.exit_code,\n        )\n\n        # Update TUI\n        self._tui.update_step(\n            step_id,\n            status=step_result.status,\n            duration=self._format_duration(duration),\n        )\n\n        # Record diagnostics\n        self._diagnostics.record_step(step_result)\n\n        # Store context summary for dependent steps (NFR-PRD.11/GAP-004)\n        self._context_summaries[step_id] = (\n            f\"{step_id}: {step_result.status.value}\"\n        )\n\n        return step_result\n\n    def _run_subprocess_step(\n        self,\n        step_id: str,\n        step_name: str,\n        builder_name: str,\n    ) -> PrdStepResult:\n        \"\"\"Run a step that requires a Claude subprocess.\"\"\"\n        # Budget guard (NFR-PRD.4)\n        turns_needed = self._estimate_turns(step_id)\n        if not self._ledger.can_allocate(turns_needed):\n            return PrdStepResult(\n                status=PrdStepStatus.QA_FAIL_EXHAUSTED,\n timeout_seconds=self._config.stall_timeout * 30,\n        )\n\n        try:\n            proc.start_with_retry()\n            exit_code = proc.wait()\n        except RuntimeError as exc:\n PrdStepStatus.HALT\n                else:\n                    status = PrdStepStatus.VALIDATION_FAIL\n                self._tui.update_step(step_id, gate_state=\"FAIL\")\n            else:\n \"\"\"Classify step outcome from exit code and output content.\n\n        NFR-PRD.3/F-007: Sentinel detection with anchored regex,\n        code block exclusion.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Timeout\n        if exit_code == 124:\n            return PrdStepStatus.TIMEOUT\n\n        # Crash\n        if exit_code != 0:\n            return PrdStepStatus.ERROR\n\n        # Sentinel detection (F-007)\n PrdStepStatus.QA_FAIL\n            if '\"verdict\": \"PASS\"' in output or \"verdict: PASS\" in output:\n                return PrdStepStatus.PASS\n\n        # No sentinel found -- pass with caveat\n        return PrdStepStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    def _evaluate_gate(\n        self,\n        step_id: str,\n        gate,\n        content: str,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Evaluate gate criteria for a step's output.\"\"\"\n        from superclaude.cli.pipeline.models import GateCriteria\n\n        # Check min lines\n        if gate.min_lines > 0:\n            line_count = len(content.splitlines())\n            if line_count < gate.min_lines:\n                self._diagnostics.record_gate_failure(\n                    step_id,\n                    f\"Insufficient lines: -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Stage B: Dynamic step generation\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _execute_stage_b(self, result: PrdPipelineResult) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Execute Stage B: dynamic investigation, QA, synthesis, assembly.\"\"\"\n        if self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n   ) -> list[tuple[str, str, str]]:\n        \"\"\"Generate dynamic investigation steps from research notes.\n\n        F-012: Step count depends on tier:\n        - lightweight: 2-3 agents\n        - -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _execute_parallel_steps(\n        self,\n        steps: list[tuple[str, str, str]],\n        result: PrdPipelineResult,\n        group_name: str,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Execute steps in parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor.\n\n        NFR-PRD.7: max_workers = min(len(steps), 10).\n        Zero-step guard: empty list -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _execute_qa_fix_cycle(\n        self,\n        result: PrdPipelineResult,\n        *,\n        qa_step_id: str,\n        qa_builder: str,\n        fix_builder: str,\n        max_cycles: int,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Execute QA -> fix -> re-QA loop.\n\n        F-006: Budget deducted from main TurnLedger. Max cycles\n        from config. Budget exhaustion mid-cycle produces\n        QA_FAIL_EXHAUSTED with partial results in resume state.\n        \"\"\"\n        for cycle in range(max_cycles + 1):  # +1 for initial QA\n            if self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                return\n\n            # Budget check before QA\n            if not self._ledger.can_allocate(10):\n                qa_result = PrdStepResult(\n                    status=PrdStepStatus.QA_FAIL_EXHAUSTED,\n                    fix_cycle=cycle,\n                )\n                self._step_results.append(qa_result)\n                result.step_results.append(qa_result)\n                result.outcome = \"halt\"\n                result.halt_step = qa_step_id\n                result.halt_reason = \"Budget exhausted during fix cycle\"\n                return\n\n            # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internal steps (no subprocess)\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _run_check_existing(self) -> PrdStepResult:\n        \"\"\"Step 1: Check for existing PRD work.\"\"\"\n        state = check_existing_work(self._config)\n\n        if state == ExistingWorkState.ALREADY_COMPLETE:\n            return PrdStepResult(status=PrdStepStatus.SKIPPED)\n\n        if state == ExistingWorkState.RESUME_STAGE_A:\n            self._context_summaries[\"check-existing\"] = (\n                \"Resuming from Stage A (task dir exists, no research)\"\n            )\n\n        if state == ExistingWorkState.RESUME_STAGE_B:\n            self._context_summaries[\"check-existing\"] = (\n                \"Resuming from Stage B (research exists, synthesis incomplete)\"\n            )\n\n        return PrdStepResult(status=PrdStepStatus.PASS)\n\n    def _run_template_triage(self) -> PrdStepResult:\n        \"\"\"Step 6: Select PRD template variant.\"\"\"\n        template_num = select_template(self._config.prd_scope)\n        self._context_summaries[\"template-triage\"] = (\n            f\"Template variant {template_num} selected for scope \"\n            f\"{self._config.prd_scope!r}\"\n        )\n        return PrdStepResult(status=PrdStepStatus.PASS)\n\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Helpers\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _estimate_turns(step_id: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Estimate turns needed for a step (conservative).\"\"\"\n        # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n# Anchored regex: must start at line beginning\n_EXIT_SENTINEL_PATTERN = re.compile(\n    r\"^EXIT_RECOMMENDATION:\\s*(CONTINUE|HALT)\", re.MULTILINE\n)\n\n# Fenced code block detection: ```...```\n_CODE_BLOCK_PATTERN = re.compile(r\"```[\\s\\S]*?```\")\n\n\ndef _detect_sentinel(output: str) -> Optional[str]:\n    \"\"\"Extract EXIT_RECOMMENDATION from subprocess output.\n\n    F-007: Uses ``^EXIT_RECOMMENDATION:`` anchored regex with\n    ``re.MULTILINE``, skipping matches inside fenced code blocks.\n\n    Returns:\n        \"CONTINUE\" or \"HALT\" if a valid sentinel is found outside\n        code blocks, None otherwise.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Remove fenced code blocks before searching\n    cleaned = _CODE_BLOCK_PATTERN.sub(\"\", output)\n\n    match = _EXIT_SENTINEL_PATTERN.search(cleaned)\n    if match:\n        return match.group(1)\n    return None\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Turn ledger (NFR-PRD.4)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass TurnLedger:\n    \"\"\"Budget tracker for subprocess turn allocation.\n\n    Guards against budget exhaustion by tracking allocated and consumed\n    turns across all subprocess launches.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    total_budget: int = 300\n    allocated: int = 0\n    consumed: int = 0\n\n    def can_allocate(self, turns: int) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if the requested turns can be allocated.\"\"\"\n        return (self.allocated + turns) <= self.total_budget\n\n    def allocate(self, turns: int) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Allocate turns from the budget. Returns False if insufficient.\"\"\"\n        if not self.can_allocate(turns):\n            return False\n        self.allocated += turns\n        return True\n\n    def consume(self, turns: int) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Record turns actually consumed by a subprocess.\"\"\"\n        self.consumed += turns\n\n    @property\n    def remaining(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Turns remaining in the budget.\"\"\"\n        return max(self.total_budget - self.allocated, 0)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Signal handler (NFR-PRD.9)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass PrdSignalHandler:\n    \"\"\"Register signal handlers for graceful PRD pipeline shutdown.\n\n    On SIGINT/SIGTERM: set the shutdown flag so the executor loop\n    can write resume state and exit gracefully.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self.shutdown_requested = False\n        self._original_sigint = None\n        self._original_sigterm = None\n\n    def install(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Install signal handlers.\"\"\"\n        self._original_sigint = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGINT)\n        self._original_sigterm = signal.getsignal(signal.SIGTERM)\n        signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._handle)\n        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self._handle)\n\n    def uninstall(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Restore original signal handlers.\"\"\"\n        if self._original_sigint is not None:\n            signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, self._original_sigint)\n        if self._original_sigterm is not None:\n            signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, self._original_sigterm)\n\n    def _handle(self, signum, frame) -> None:\n        self.shutdown_requested = True\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Step definitions\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# PrdExecutor\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass PrdExecutor:\n    \"\"\"Main PRD pipeline executor.\n\n    Drives the 15-step pipeline through sequential Stage A, dynamic\n    parallel Stage B, and final completion step.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, config: PrdConfig) -> None:\n        self._config = config\n        self._ledger = TurnLedger(total_budget=config.max_turns)\n        self._monitor = PrdMonitor()\n        self._logger = PrdTUI()\n        self._signal_handler = PrdSignalHandler()\n        self._step_results: list[PrdStepResult] = []\n        self._context_summaries: dict[str, str] = {}\n\n    def run(self) -> PrdPipelineResult:\n        \"\"\"Execute the full PRD pipeline.\n\n        Returns:\n            PrdPipelineResult with all step outcomes.\n        \"\"\"\n        result = PrdPipelineResult(config=self._config)\n\n        # Dry-run mode: validate config only\n        if self._config.dry_run:\n            result.outcome = \"dry_run\"\n            result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n            return result\n\n        # Install signal handlers (NFR-PRD.9)\n        self._signal_handler.install()\n\n        try:\n            # Create task directories\n            create_task_dirs(self._config.task_dir)\n\n            # Register steps in TUI\n            all_step_ids = [\n                (s[0], s[1]) for s in _STAGE_A_STEPS\n            ]\n            self._tui.register_steps(all_step_ids)\n            self._tui.start()\n\n            # Stage A: Sequential steps 1-9\n            for step_id, step_name, builder_name, _ in _STAGE_A_STEPS:\n                if self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    self._handle_shutdown(result)\n                    return result\n\n                step_result = self._execute_step(\n                    step_id, step_name, builder_name\n                )\n                self._step_results.append(step_result)\n                result.step_results.append(step_result)\n\n                # STRICT gate failure halts pipeline\n                if step_result.status.is_failure:\n                    gate = GATE_CRITERIA.get(step_id)\n                    if gate and gate.enforcement_tier == \"STRICT\":\n                        result.outcome = \"halt\"\n                        result.halt_step = step_id\n                        result.halt_reason = (\n                            f\"STRICT gate failure: {step_result.status.value}\"\n                        )\n                        break\n\n            # Stage B: Dynamic steps 10-14 (only if Stage A succeeded)\n            if result.outcome != \"halt\":\n                self._execute_stage_b(result)\n\n            # Step 15: Completion (only if not halted)\n            if result.outcome != \"halt\":\n                if not self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    completion_result = self._execute_step(\n                        \"present-complete\",\n                        \"Present & Complete\",\n                        \"build_completion_prompt\",\n                    )\n                    self._step_results.append(completion_result)\n                    result.step_results.append(completion_result)\n\n            # Finalize\n            if result.outcome != \"halt\":\n                result.outcome = \"success\"\n            result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        finally:\n            self._signal_handler.uninstall()\n            self._tui.stop()\n\n        return result\n\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Step execution\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _execute_step(\n        self,\n        step_id: str,\n        step_name: str,\n        builder_name: str,\n    ) -> PrdStepResult:\n        \"\"\"Execute a single pipeline step.\"\"\"\n        self._logger.log_step_start(step_id, step_name)\n        self._tui.update_step(\n            step_id, status=PrdStepStatus.RUNNING, gate_state=\"PENDING\"\n        )\n\n        start_time = time.monotonic()\n        step_result = PrdStepResult(status=PrdStepStatus.RUNNING)\n\n        # Handle internal steps (no subprocess needed)\n        if builder_name == \"_check_existing\":\n            step_result = self._run_check_existing()\n        elif builder_name == \"_template_triage\":\n            step_result = self._run_template_triage()\n        else:\n            step_result = self._run_subprocess_step(\n                step_id, step_name, builder_name\n            )\n\n        duration = time.monotonic() - start_time\n\n        # Log completion\n        self._logger.log_step_complete(\n            step_id,\n            step_result.status.value,\n            duration_seconds=duration,\n            exit_code=step_result.exit_code,\n        )\n\n        # Update TUI\n        self._tui.update_step(\n            step_id,\n            status=step_result.status,\n            duration=self._format_duration(duration),\n        )\n\n        # Record diagnostics\n        self._diagnostics.record_step(step_result)\n\n        # Store context summary for dependent steps (NFR-PRD.11/GAP-004)\n        self._context_summaries[step_id] = (\n            f\"{step_id}: {step_result.status.value}\"\n        )\n\n        return step_result\n\n    def _run_subprocess_step(\n        self,\n        step_id: str,\n        step_name: str,\n        builder_name: str,\n    ) -> PrdStepResult:\n        \"\"\"Run a step that requires a Claude subprocess.\"\"\"\n        # Budget guard (NFR-PRD.4)\n        turns_needed = self._estimate_turns(step_id)\n        if not self._ledger.can_allocate(turns_needed):\n            return PrdStepResult(\n                status=PrdStepStatus.QA_FAIL_EXHAUSTED,\n                exit_code=-1,\n            )\n        self._ledger.allocate(turns_needed)\n\n        # Build prompt\n        prompt = self._build_prompt(builder_name)\n\n        # Create output files\n        output_file = self._config.task_dir / f\"{step_id}-output.txt\"\n        error_file = self._config.task_dir / f\"{step_id}-error.txt\"\n\n        # Create and launch subprocess\n        proc = PrdClaudeProcess(\n            config=self._config,\n            step_id=step_id,\n            prompt=prompt,\n            output_file=output_file,\n            error_file=error_file,\n            timeout_seconds=self._config.stall_timeout * 30,\n        )\n\n        try:\n            proc.start_with_retry()\n            exit_code = proc.wait()\n        except RuntimeError as exc:\n            return PrdStepResult(\n                status=PrdStepStatus.ERROR,\n                exit_code=-1,\n            )\n\n        # Read output for sentinel detection\n        output_text = \"\"\n        try:\n            output_text = output_file.read_text(\n                encoding=\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\"\n            )\n        except OSError:\n            pass\n\n        # Determine status\n        status = self._determine_status(exit_code, output_text, step_id)\n\n        # Gate evaluation\n        gate = GATE_CRITERIA.get(step_id)\n        if gate and status.is_success:\n            gate_passed = self._evaluate_gate(step_id, gate, output_text)\n            if not gate_passed:\n                if gate.enforcement_tier == \"STRICT\":\n                    status = PrdStepStatus.HALT\n                else:\n                    status = PrdStepStatus.VALIDATION_FAIL\n                self._tui.update_step(step_id, gate_state=\"FAIL\")\n            else:\n                self._tui.update_step(step_id, gate_state=\"PASS\")\n\n        return PrdStepResult(\n            status=status,\n            exit_code=exit_code,\n            output_bytes=len(output_text.encode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")),\n        )\n\n    def _determine_status(\n        self, exit_code: int, output: str, step_id: str\n    ) -> PrdStepStatus:\n        \"\"\"Classify step outcome from exit code and output content.\n\n        NFR-PRD.3/F-007: Sentinel detection with anchored regex,\n        code block exclusion.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Timeout\n        if exit_code == 124:\n            return PrdStepStatus.TIMEOUT\n\n        # Crash\n        if exit_code != 0:\n            return PrdStepStatus.ERROR\n\n        # Sentinel detection (F-007)\n        sentinel = _detect_sentinel(output)\n        if sentinel == \"HALT\":\n            return PrdStepStatus.HALT\n        if sentinel == \"CONTINUE\":\n            return PrdStepStatus.PASS\n\n        # QA steps: check for verdict\n        if \"qa\" in step_id or \"review\" in step_id:\n            if '\"verdict\": \"FAIL\"' in output or \"verdict: FAIL\" in output:\n                return PrdStepStatus.QA_FAIL\n            if '\"verdict\": \"PASS\"' in output or \"verdict: PASS\" in output:\n                return PrdStepStatus.PASS\n\n        # No sentinel found -- pass with caveat\n        return PrdStepStatus.PASS_NO_SIGNAL\n\n    def _evaluate_gate(\n        self,\n        step_id: str,\n        gate,\n        content: str,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Evaluate gate criteria for a step's output.\"\"\"\n        from superclaude.cli.pipeline.models import GateCriteria\n\n        # Check min lines\n        if gate.min_lines > 0:\n            line_count = len(content.splitlines())\n            if line_count < gate.min_lines:\n                self._diagnostics.record_gate_failure(\n                    step_id,\n                    f\"Insufficient lines: {line_count} < {gate.min_lines}\",\n                    gate.enforcement_tier,\n                )\n                self._logger.log_gate_result(\n                    step_id, False,\n                    f\"Min lines: {line_count}/{gate.min_lines}\",\n                )\n                return False\n\n        # Run semantic checks\n        if gate.semantic_checks:\n            for check in gate.semantic_checks:\n                result = check.check_fn(content)\n                if result is not True:\n                    msg = result if isinstance(result, str) else check.failure_message\n                    self._diagnostics.record_gate_failure(\n                        step_id, msg, gate.enforcement_tier\n                    )\n                    self._logger.log_gate_result(step_id, False, msg)\n                    return False\n\n        self._logger.log_gate_result(step_id, True, \"All checks passed\")\n        return True\n\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Stage B: Dynamic step generation\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _execute_stage_b(self, result: PrdPipelineResult) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Execute Stage B: dynamic investigation, QA, synthesis, assembly.\"\"\"\n        if self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n            return\n\n        # Step 10: Investigation (parallel)\n        research_files = discover_research_files(self._config.task_dir)\n        investigation_steps = self._build_investigation_steps()\n\n        if investigation_steps:\n            self._execute_parallel_steps(\n                investigation_steps, result, \"investigation\"\n            )\n            result.research_agent_count = len(investigation_steps)\n\n        # Step 11: Research QA + fix cycle\n        if result.outcome != \"halt\" and not self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n            self._execute_qa_fix_cycle(\n                result,\n                qa_step_id=\"research-qa\",\n                qa_builder=\"build_qa_research_gate_prompt\",\n                fix_builder=\"build_gap_filling_prompt\",\n                max_cycles=self._config.max_research_fix_cycles,\n            )\n\n        # Step 12: Web research (parallel, if needed)\n        if result.outcome != \"halt\" and not self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n            web_steps = self._build_web_research_steps()\n            if web_steps:\n                self._execute_parallel_steps(\n                    web_steps, result, \"web-research\"\n                )\n                result.web_agent_count = len(web_steps)\n\n        # Step 13a: Synthesis (parallel)\n        if result.outcome != \"halt\" and not self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n            synth_steps = self._build_synthesis_steps()\n            if synth_steps:\n                self._execute_parallel_steps(\n                    synth_steps, result, \"synthesis\"\n                )\n                result.synthesis_agent_count = len(synth_steps)\n\n        # Step 13b: Synthesis QA + fix cycle\n        if result.outcome != \"halt\" and not self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n            self._execute_qa_fix_cycle(\n                result,\n                qa_step_id=\"synthesis-qa\",\n                qa_builder=\"build_qa_synthesis_gate_prompt\",\n                fix_builder=\"build_gap_filling_prompt\",\n                max_cycles=self._config.max_synthesis_fix_cycles,\n            )\n\n        # Step 14a: Assembly\n        if result.outcome != \"halt\" and not self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n            assembly_result = self._execute_step(\n                \"assembly\", \"Assembly\", \"build_assembly_prompt\"\n            )\n            self._step_results.append(assembly_result)\n            result.step_results.append(assembly_result)\n\n            if assembly_result.status.is_failure:\n                gate = GATE_CRITERIA.get(\"assembly\")\n                if gate and gate.enforcement_tier == \"STRICT\":\n                    result.outcome = \"halt\"\n                    result.halt_step = \"assembly\"\n                    return\n\n        # Step 14b: Structural QA\n        if result.outcome != \"halt\" and not self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n            struct_qa = self._execute_step(\n                \"structural-qa\", \"Structural QA\", \"build_qualitative_qa_prompt\"\n            )\n            self._step_results.append(qual_qa)\n            result.step_results.append(qual_qa)\n\n    def _build_investigation_steps(\n        self,\n    ) -> list[tuple[str, str, str]]:\n        \"\"\"Generate dynamic investigation steps from research notes.\n\n        F-012: Step count depends on tier:\n        - lightweight: 2-3 agents\n        - standard: 4-6 agents\n        - heavyweight: 6-10 agents\n        \"\"\"\n        tier = self._config.tier\n        if tier == \"lightweight\":\n            agent_count = 3\n        elif tier == \"heavyweight\":\n            agent_count = 8\n        else:  # standard\n            agent_count = 5\n\n        steps = []\n        for i in range(agent_count):\n            step_id = f\"investigation-{i + 1}\"\n            step_name = f\"Investigation Agent {i + 1}\"\n            steps.append((step_id, step_name, \"build_investigation_prompt\"))\n\n        return steps\n\n    def _build_web_research_steps(self) -> list[tuple[str, str, str]]:\n        \"\"\"Generate web research steps based on tier.\"\"\"\n        tier = self._config.tier\n        if tier == \"lightweight\":\n            count = 1\n        elif tier == \"heavyweight\":\n            count = 3\n        else:\n            count = 2\n\n        return [\n            (\n                f\"web-research-{i + 1}\",\n                f\"Web Research Agent {i + 1}\",\n                \"build_web_research_prompt\",\n            )\n            for i in range(count)\n        ]\n\n    def _build_synthesis_steps(self) -> list[tuple[str, str, str]]:\n        \"\"\"Generate synthesis steps from mapping table.\"\"\"\n        mapping = load_synthesis_mapping(self._config.skill_refs_dir)\n        return [\n            (\n                f\"synthesis-{i + 1}\",\n                f\"Synthesis: {entry['synth_file']}\",\n                \"build_synthesis_prompt\",\n            )\n            for i, entry in enumerate(mapping)\n        ]\n\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Parallel execution\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _execute_parallel_steps(\n        self,\n        steps: list[tuple[str, str, str]],\n        result: PrdPipelineResult,\n        group_name: str,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Execute steps in parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor.\n\n        NFR-PRD.7: max_workers = min(len(steps), 10).\n        Zero-step guard: empty list returns immediately.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not steps:\n            return\n\n        max_workers = min(len(steps), 10)\n\n        with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_workers) as pool:\n            futures = {}\n            for step_id, step_name, builder in steps:\n                if self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                    break\n                future = pool.submit(\n                    self._execute_step, step_id, step_name, builder\n                )\n                futures[future] = step_id\n\n            for future in as_completed(futures):\n                step_id = futures[future]\n                try:\n                    step_result = future.result()\n                except Exception:\n                    step_result = PrdStepResult(\n                        status=PrdStepStatus.ERROR,\n                        exit_code=-1,\n                    )\n                self._step_results.append(step_result)\n                result.step_results.append(step_result)\n\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Fix cycle loop (F-006)\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _execute_qa_fix_cycle(\n        self,\n        result: PrdPipelineResult,\n        *,\n        qa_step_id: str,\n        qa_builder: str,\n        fix_builder: str,\n        max_cycles: int,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Execute QA -> fix -> re-QA loop.\n\n        F-006: Budget deducted from main TurnLedger. Max cycles\n        from config. Budget exhaustion mid-cycle produces\n        QA_FAIL_EXHAUSTED with partial results in resume state.\n        \"\"\"\n        for cycle in range(max_cycles + 1):  # +1 for initial QA\n            if self._signal_handler.shutdown_requested:\n                return\n\n            # Budget check before QA\n            if not self._ledger.can_allocate(10):\n                qa_result = PrdStepResult(\n                    status=PrdStepStatus.QA_FAIL_EXHAUSTED,\n                    fix_cycle=cycle,\n                )\n                self._step_results.append(qa_result)\n                result.step_results.append(qa_result)\n                result.outcome = \"halt\"\n                result.halt_step = qa_step_id\n                result.halt_reason = \"Budget exhausted during fix cycle\"\n                return\n\n            # Run QA\n            qa_result = self._execute_step(\n                qa_step_id,\n                f\"QA ({qa_step_id}, cycle {cycle})\",\n                qa_builder,\n            )\n            self._step_results.append(qa_result)\n            result.step_results.append(qa_result)\n\n            self._diagnostics.record_fix_cycle(\n                qa_step_id, cycle, qa_result.status.value\n            )\n\n            # QA passed -> done\n            if qa_result.status.is_success:\n                if qa_step_id == \"research-qa\":\n                    result.research_fix_cycles = cycle\n                else:\n                    result.synthesis_fix_cycles = cycle\n                return\n\n            # QA failed but not a fix cycle trigger -> halt\n            if not qa_result.status.needs_fix_cycle:\n                if qa_result.status.is_failure:\n                    gate = GATE_CRITERIA.get(qa_step_id)\n                    if gate and gate.enforcement_tier == \"STRICT\":\n                        result.outcome = \"halt\"\n                        result.halt_step = qa_step_id\n                        result.halt_reason = (\n                            f\"QA failure: {qa_result.status.value}\"\n                        )\n                return\n\n            # Last cycle exhausted -> halt with QA_FAIL_EXHAUSTED\n            if cycle >= max_cycles:\n                result.outcome = \"halt\"\n                result.halt_step = qa_step_id\n                result.halt_reason = \"Fix cycles exhausted\"\n                return\n\n            # Spawn gap-fillers and re-QA\n            self._tui.update_step(\n                qa_step_id,\n                fix_cycle=cycle + 1,\n                qa_verdict=\"FAIL\",\n            )\n\n            fix_result = self._execute_step(\n                f\"{qa_step_id}-fix-{cycle + 1}\",\n                f\"Gap Fill ({qa_step_id}, cycle {cycle + 1})\",\n                fix_builder,\n            )\n            self._step_results.append(fix_result)\n            result.step_results.append(fix_result)\n\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internal steps (no subprocess)\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _run_check_existing(self) -> PrdStepResult:\n        \"\"\"Step 1: Check for existing PRD work.\"\"\"\n        state = check_existing_work(self._config)\n\n        if state == ExistingWorkState.ALREADY_COMPLETE:\n            return PrdStepResult(status=PrdStepStatus.SKIPPED)\n\n        if state == ExistingWorkState.RESUME_STAGE_A:\n            self._context_summaries[\"check-existing\"] = (\n                \"Resuming from Stage A (task dir exists, no research)\"\n            )\n\n        if state == ExistingWorkState.RESUME_STAGE_B:\n            self._context_summaries[\"check-existing\"] = (\n                \"Resuming from Stage B (research exists, synthesis incomplete)\"\n            )\n\n        return PrdStepResult(status=PrdStepStatus.PASS)\n\n    def _run_template_triage(self) -> PrdStepResult:\n        \"\"\"Step 6: Select PRD template variant.\"\"\"\n        template_num = select_template(self._config.prd_scope)\n        self._context_summaries[\"template-triage\"] = (\n            f\"Template variant {template_num} selected for scope \"\n            f\"{self._config.prd_scope!r}\"\n        )\n        return PrdStepResult(status=PrdStepStatus.PASS)\n\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Prompt building\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _build_prompt(self, builder_name: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Build a prompt by calling the named builder function.\n\n        NFR-PRD.11/GAP-004: Injects context summaries from prior steps.\n        Verbose summaries for direct dependencies, terse for transitive.\n        \"\"\"\n        from . import prompts\n\n        builder_fn = getattr(prompts, builder_name, None)\n        if builder_fn is None:\n            return f\"Execute step using builder {builder_name}\"\n\n        # Collect context summaries from prior steps\n        summaries = list(self._context_summaries.values())\n\n        try:\n            return builder_fn(\n                self._config,\n                context_summaries=summaries if summaries else None,\n            )\n        except TypeError:\n            # Builder doesn't accept context_summaries\n            return builder_fn(self._config)\n\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Shutdown handling (NFR-PRD.9)\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def _handle_shutdown(self, result: PrdPipelineResult) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle graceful shutdown: preserve state for resume.\"\"\"\n        result.outcome = \"interrupted\"\n        result.finished_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Find last completed step\n        completed = [\n            r for r in self._step_results\n            if r.status.is_terminal\n        ]\n        if completed:\n            last = completed[-1]\n            last_step = getattr(last.step, \"name\", \"unknown\") if last.step else \"unknown\"\n            result.halt_step = last_step\n            result.halt_reason = \"Signal-interrupted shutdown\"\n\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Helpers\n    # -------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _estimate_turns(step_id: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Estimate turns needed for a step (conservative).\"\"\"\n        # QA and verification steps are lighter\n        if \"qa\" in step_id or \"verify\" in step_id or \"review\" in step_id:\n            return 10\n        # Assembly and task file building are heavier\n        if \"assembly\" in step_id or \"build\" in step_id:\n            return 30\n        # Investigation and synthesis are medium\n        if \"investigation\" in step_id or \"synthesis\" in step_id:\n            return 20\n        # Default\n        return 15\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _format_duration(seconds: float) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format duration for TUI display.\"\"\"\n        if seconds < 60:\n            return f\"{seconds:.1f}s\"\n        minutes = int(seconds // 60)\n        secs = seconds % 60\n        return f\"{minutes}m{secs:.0f}s\"\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
